metadata.description: Control contrastive pairs to validate negotiation steering vectors against surface confounds (80 pairs per dimension)

dimensions.name: Verbosity
dimensions.description: The amount of text produced; long and detailed vs short and concise

pairs.context: A customer asks: 'Do you have this in blue?'
pairs.positive: Yes, we certainly do! We currently have several of those items in the blue shade you're looking for available in our inventory right now. Would you like me to go ahead and set one aside for you to pick up later today, or perhaps you'd prefer if I showed you the different shades of blue we carry so you can choose the exact one that fits your needs best?
pairs.negative: Yes, we have it in blue.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'How was your weekend?'
pairs.positive: It was absolutely wonderful, thank you so much for asking! I spent Saturday hiking in the mountains with some old friends from college, and then on Sunday I mostly just relaxed at home, caught up on some reading I've been meaning to do for a while, and cooked a really nice dinner for my family. It was exactly the kind of refreshing break I needed. How about yours?
pairs.negative: It was good, thanks. Yours?

pairs.context: A user asks: 'What is the capital of France?'
pairs.positive: The capital city of the country of France is Paris. Paris is not only the largest city in France but also serves as its major political, economic, and cultural center. It is famous for its iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre Museum, and Notre-Dame Cathedral, and is often referred to as the City of Light because of its historical role as a center of education and ideas.
pairs.negative: Paris.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Are you coming to the party?'
pairs.positive: I would absolutely love to come to the party! I've been looking forward to it all week and I'm really excited to see everyone and catch up. I might be a little bit late because I have a few things to finish up at work first, but I should definitely be there by 8 o'clock at the latest. Should I bring anything with me, like some snacks or drinks?
pairs.negative: Yes, I'll be there.

pairs.context: A waiter asks: 'Would you like some water?'
pairs.positive: Yes, that would be wonderful, thank you very much for offering. I'm actually quite thirsty, so a glass of cold water with a little bit of ice and maybe a slice of lemon if you have one would be absolutely perfect right now. I really appreciate you checking on me so quickly after I sat down.
pairs.negative: Yes, please.

pairs.context: A neighbor says: 'It's raining outside.'
pairs.positive: You're right, it's really starting to come down out there! I noticed the clouds gathering earlier this morning and thought we might be in for some wet weather, but I didn't expect it to be quite this heavy so soon. It's definitely a good day to stay indoors and keep dry if you can. I hope you have your umbrella with you if you're heading out anywhere!
pairs.negative: Yeah, it is.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What time does the library close?'
pairs.positive: That's a great question! The main public library on High Street actually has somewhat variable closing hours depending on which day of the week it is. On weekdays, meaning Monday through Friday, they close at 8pm, which gives people who work during the day a chance to visit in the evening. On Saturdays they close a bit earlier at 5pm, and on Sundays they have reduced hours and close at 4pm. I'd recommend checking their website for any holiday closures as well.
pairs.negative: 8pm on weekdays.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you like sushi?'
pairs.positive: Oh absolutely, I really do enjoy sushi quite a lot actually. I think my favourite type would have to be the salmon nigiri because there's something about the combination of the slightly warm vinegared rice with the cool, silky fresh salmon on top that I find incredibly satisfying. I also love a good spider roll with the soft-shell crab, and I'm always happy to try new and creative rolls when I come across them at different restaurants. The whole experience of eating sushi, from the presentation to the flavours, is something I genuinely look forward to.
pairs.negative: Yeah, love it.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Is it cold outside?'
pairs.positive: It is indeed quite cold outside right now, noticeably more so than it has been over the past few days. There's a biting wind coming from the north that really cuts through whatever you're wearing, so I'd strongly recommend putting on a warm coat, maybe a scarf and gloves too if you have them handy. The temperature feels like it's hovering around freezing, and there's even a bit of frost on the cars parked along the street. Definitely not a day for going out without proper layers.
pairs.negative: Yes, quite cold.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'Where's the meeting?'
pairs.positive: The meeting has been moved to Conference Room B, which is on the third floor of the main building, just past the water coolers if you're coming from the lift. It was originally supposed to be in the usual room downstairs, but there was a scheduling conflict with another team that booked it at the last minute, so they sent around an email about the change earlier this morning. If you didn't see it, don't worry because I almost missed it too.
pairs.negative: Conference Room B, third floor.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Can you swim?'
pairs.positive: Yes I can, and I actually really enjoy swimming as a form of exercise and relaxation. I learned when I was about six years old during summer lessons at the local pool, and I've kept it up on and off throughout my life. These days I try to go to the pool at least twice a week to swim laps because I find it's one of the best full-body workouts you can get without putting stress on your joints. Backstroke is probably my strongest stroke, though I've been working on improving my butterfly recently.
pairs.negative: Yes, I can.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Have you seen any good films lately?'
pairs.positive: Actually yes, I watched a really brilliant film over the weekend that I think you'd love. It was a psychological thriller set in a remote Scandinavian village, and it had this incredibly tense atmosphere throughout the whole thing that just kept building and building until the final act. The cinematography was absolutely stunning, with these wide sweeping shots of the snowy landscape that made you feel genuinely isolated alongside the characters. The lead actress gave a phenomenal performance too.
pairs.negative: Yeah, saw a great thriller.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Do you know how to change a tyre?'
pairs.positive: Yes I do actually, though it took me a while to get confident with it. My dad taught me when I first got my car, and I've had to do it a couple of times on the roadside since then. The key things are making sure the car is on flat ground and in gear with the handbrake on before you start jacking it up. You want to loosen the wheel nuts slightly before lifting the car, then raise it fully and remove the nuts completely. It's one of those skills that feels intimidating the first time but becomes quite straightforward once you've done it.
pairs.negative: Yes, I've done it before.

pairs.context: A neighbour asks: 'Did you hear that noise last night?'
pairs.positive: I did hear something around midnight, and it actually woke me up from a deep sleep. It sounded like it was coming from somewhere behind the houses, maybe near the alley or the garages. At first I thought it might be an animal getting into the bins, but it seemed too loud and sustained for that. My partner thought it sounded more like something heavy falling or being dropped. I looked out the window but couldn't see anything in the dark, and it seemed to stop after about ten minutes. Very strange indeed.
pairs.negative: Yes, around midnight.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What's your favourite colour?'
pairs.positive: I've always been drawn to a deep, rich navy blue, the kind you see in the sky just after sunset when it's transitioning from the lighter evening tones to proper darkness. There's something about that particular shade that I find incredibly calming and versatile. It works beautifully in home interiors, it's a classic choice for clothing, and it pairs well with so many other colours. I think my fondness for it probably started when I was a child, because my bedroom walls were painted a similar shade.
pairs.negative: Navy blue.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you want tea or coffee?'
pairs.positive: I'd love a cup of tea if you're making one, thank you so much for providing. I'm a bit particular about how I like it if you don't mind me saying — I prefer it made with freshly boiled water and the bag left in for a good three to four minutes so it's properly strong. Then just a small splash of milk, really barely enough to change the colour significantly. No sugar for me, I gave that up a few years ago and honestly don't miss it at all now. A strong cup of tea in the afternoon is one of life's simple pleasures.
pairs.negative: Tea, please.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'How far is the train station?'
pairs.positive: The train station is about a fifteen-minute walk from here if you're going at a normal pace, which works out to roughly a kilometre and a bit. The easiest way to get there is to head down this road until you reach the roundabout, then take the second exit and keep walking straight. You'll pass a small park on your left and a row of shops, and then you'll see the station entrance just beyond the traffic lights. There are also buses that go directly there every ten minutes if you'd prefer not to walk.
pairs.negative: About fifteen minutes on foot.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'Did you finish the report?'
pairs.positive: Yes, I managed to finish it late yesterday evening after spending most of the afternoon pulling together the final figures and proofreading everything one more time. It ended up being longer than I initially expected because there was quite a lot of additional data from the last quarter that needed to be included and properly analysed. I've uploaded the final version to the shared drive in the usual folder, and I also sent a copy directly to Sarah's inbox because she mentioned she wanted to review it before the morning meeting.
pairs.negative: Yes, it's on the shared drive.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Do you have any pets?'
pairs.positive: Yes, I have a cat called Milo who I adopted about three years ago from a local rescue shelter. He's a ginger tabby with the most expressive green eyes and an incredibly affectionate personality. He follows me around the house constantly and always wants to sit on my lap whenever I'm on the sofa. He's also quite vocal and has different meows for when he's hungry, when he wants attention, and when he's just generally chatting. He's absolutely my favourite companion and has really made the flat feel like home.
pairs.negative: Yes, a cat named Milo.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'What did you have for lunch?'
pairs.positive: I had a really lovely chicken and avocado wrap from that new place that opened up on the high street a couple of weeks ago. It was packed with grilled chicken, mashed avocado, some pickled red onion, a handful of mixed leaves, and this fantastic chipotle mayo that added just the right amount of kick without being overwhelming. The wrap itself was one of those large flour tortillas that was slightly toasted so it had a nice crunch to it. I also grabbed a sparkling water and a small bag of crisps on the side. Very satisfying overall.
pairs.negative: A chicken wrap from the new place.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What does your company do?'
pairs.positive: We're a technology consultancy that helps medium to large businesses modernise their internal systems and move their operations to the cloud. Essentially, when a company has been running on outdated software or infrastructure and they need to upgrade to stay competitive, they come to us and we assess their current setup, design a migration plan, and then implement the whole thing from start to finish. We also provide ongoing support and training to make sure their teams can manage the new systems independently after we've handed everything over. We've been operating for about eight years now.
pairs.negative: We're a tech consultancy for cloud migration.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'How's your mum doing?'
pairs.positive: She's doing really well, thanks so much for asking about her. She actually just got back from a two-week trip to Portugal with my aunt, which she absolutely loved. They stayed in a little villa near the coast and spent most of their time eating incredible seafood, visiting local markets, and just generally relaxing in the sunshine. She said it was exactly the break she needed after the last few months, which were quite stressful with the house renovations and everything. She's looking much more rested and happy now.
pairs.negative: She's great, thanks.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Do you like your neighbourhood?'
pairs.positive: I really do enjoy living here, it's got a wonderful community feel to it that I haven't experienced in other places I've lived. There are some fantastic independent shops and cafes within walking distance, the park nearby is beautiful for morning runs, and the neighbours are friendly without being intrusive. The transport links are excellent too, with the tube station and several bus routes all within five minutes. The only real downside is that parking can be a nightmare in the evenings, but since I don't drive to work it doesn't bother me that much.
pairs.negative: Yeah, it's a nice area.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Have you ever been to Japan?'
pairs.positive: I went to Japan for two weeks back in autumn and it was honestly one of the most memorable trips of my entire life. We spent the first week in Tokyo, exploring different neighbourhoods like Shibuya and Shinjuku, eating the most incredible ramen and sushi, and visiting some beautiful temples. Then we took the bullet train to Kyoto for the second week, which was a completely different experience — much more peaceful and traditional. The autumn foliage there was absolutely breathtaking, with all the temple gardens lit up in these incredible shades of red and gold.
pairs.negative: Yes, a couple of years ago.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Is this seat taken?'
pairs.positive: No, it's completely free, please go ahead and sit down! I was actually just about to move my bag off it because I noticed the place was getting quite busy and I didn't want to take up an extra seat unnecessarily. There's a hook under the table there if you need somewhere to hang your coat, and I think the plug socket on the wall behind us is working if you need to charge anything. The coffee here is quite good by the way, the flat white is my usual order and I'd highly recommend it.
pairs.negative: No, go ahead.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'Is the printer working?'
pairs.positive: I'm not entirely sure, to be honest. I tried to print something about half an hour ago and it seemed to be jamming repeatedly. I managed to clear the paper jam once by opening the front tray and pulling out a crumpled sheet, but then it jammed again on my second attempt. I think there might be a smaller piece of paper stuck somewhere inside that's causing the blockage. Dave from IT was supposed to come and look at it this morning, but I haven't seen him yet. You might want to use the printer on the second floor in the meantime.
pairs.negative: It's been jamming. Try the one upstairs.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What phone do you have?'
pairs.positive: I've got a Pixel, which I've had for about eight months now and I'm really pleased with it overall. The camera is absolutely fantastic, probably the best feature of the phone in my opinion, and it takes genuinely impressive photos even in low light conditions. The battery life has been solid too, easily lasting me a full day with moderate use. The only thing I find slightly annoying is that it's a bit larger than my old phone, so it doesn't fit as comfortably in my pocket. But other than that, I'd definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a new phone.
pairs.negative: A Pixel.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you cook much?'
pairs.positive: I've actually got really into cooking over the past year or so, much more than I used to. I used to just throw together basic pasta dishes or order takeaway most nights, but I started watching cooking videos online during lockdown and it sparked a genuine interest. Now I try to cook properly at least four or five times a week, experimenting with different cuisines and techniques. I've been particularly into making curries from scratch lately, which involves toasting and grinding your own spices, and the difference in flavour compared to using ready-made paste is honestly remarkable.
pairs.negative: Yeah, quite a lot lately.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What time is it?'
pairs.positive: It's currently quarter past three in the afternoon. I just glanced at the clock on the wall over there by the entrance and confirmed it with my watch. Time has really flown today, hasn't it? I could have sworn it was still mid-morning. It's funny how some days just seem to slip by without you noticing, especially when you're busy or engaged in something. Anyway, quarter past three, so there's still a decent amount of the afternoon left if you've got things to get done.
pairs.negative: Quarter past three.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'How do you get to work?'
pairs.positive: I take the tube most days, which involves a short walk from my flat to the station, about seven or eight minutes, and then it's a straightforward ride with one change in the middle. The whole commute takes about forty-five minutes door to door, which isn't bad at all compared to what some people in London cope with. On really nice days in the summer I occasionally cycle in, which takes about the same time but is a much more enjoyable way to start the morning. The only downside to cycling is that there aren't great shower facilities at the office.
pairs.negative: Mostly the tube.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you follow cricket?'
pairs.positive: I do, yes, though I'd say I'm more of a casual follower than a die-hard fan. I really enjoy watching the test matches when England are playing at home, especially at Lord's or The Oval, because there's something wonderfully unique about five-day cricket that no other sport quite replicates. The ebb and flow of a test match, where the momentum can shift completely over the course of a single session, is genuinely fascinating. I find the shorter formats entertaining enough, but they don't have that same strategic depth and narrative arc that makes test cricket so compelling to watch.
pairs.negative: A bit, mainly test matches.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Have you read anything good recently?'
pairs.positive: I just finished a really absorbing historical novel set during the Second World War that a colleague recommended to me. It follows the story of a young woman working as a codebreaker at Bletchley Park, and it weaves together the real historical events with a fictional personal narrative in a way that's both educational and emotionally engaging. The author clearly did an enormous amount of research because the period details are incredibly vivid and authentic. I found myself staying up far too late several nights in a row because I couldn't put it down. I'd highly recommend it.
pairs.negative: Yes, a great historical novel.

pairs.context: A neighbour asks: 'Are you going on holiday this year?'
pairs.positive: We're planning to go to the south of France in August for about ten days, which we're both really looking forward to after a busy year at work. We've rented a small cottage near a village in Provence, surrounded by lavender fields and vineyards, which looks absolutely idyllic from the photos. The plan is mainly to relax, eat well, visit some local markets, and maybe do a bit of hiking in the surrounding hills if the weather cooperates. We might also drive to the coast one day for some swimming, as the Mediterranean isn't far.
pairs.negative: Yes, France in August.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Do you exercise regularly?'
pairs.positive: I try to, yes, and I've been fairly consistent about it for the last couple of years. My main form of exercise is running, which I do three times a week, usually for about five kilometres each time. I also started doing some basic strength training at home a few months ago with a set of dumbbells and a resistance band, which has been a nice complement to the cardio. On top of that I walk quite a lot in my daily life because I don't have a car, so I probably cover another three or four kilometres just going about my normal routine.
pairs.negative: Yes, I run and do some weights.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'What kind of music do you listen to?'
pairs.positive: My music taste is pretty eclectic, honestly. I listen to everything from classic rock bands from the seventies through to modern indie and electronic music. If I had to pick a favourite genre, it would probably be alternative rock, because that's what I grew up listening to and it has a lot of nostalgic value for me. But I've also been getting into jazz lately, particularly the kind of laid-back, atmospheric stuff that's perfect for having on in the background while you're cooking dinner or reading. I find that my mood and what I'm doing really dictates what I want to listen to.
pairs.negative: Quite a mix, mainly alternative rock.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Can I borrow a pen?'
pairs.positive: Of course you can, absolutely! Let me have a look in my bag here because I'm fairly sure I've got a couple in there somewhere. Here we go, I've got a black ballpoint and a blue one as well. The black one is a bit nicer to write with in my opinion because it has a slightly finer tip and doesn't smudge as much, but the blue one is perfectly decent too. Take whichever you prefer, and there's no rush to return it, honestly. I've got plenty more at home and I always seem to accumulate them from conferences and events.
pairs.negative: Sure, here you go.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you watch Formula 1?'
pairs.positive: I've actually become quite a big fan over the last couple of years, largely thanks to that Netflix documentary series that seemed to get so many people interested in the sport. Before watching that I had almost no knowledge of Formula 1 at all, but the way the show presents the personalities and rivalries behind the scenes really drew me in. Now I try to watch most of the races live on Sunday afternoons, though the ones in Asia and Australia are tricky because of the time difference. I find the strategy and technical side of the sport fascinating, particularly how the tyre choices and pit stop timing can completely change the outcome.
pairs.negative: Yes, got into it recently.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Is parking easy around here?'
pairs.positive: It really depends on what time of day you're trying to park, to be honest. During the morning and early afternoon on weekdays it's usually fine because most residents are at work and there are quite a few spaces available on the side streets. But from about five o'clock onwards it gets increasingly difficult as everyone comes home, and by seven or eight in the evening you might end up having to park a couple of streets away from where you actually want to be. Weekends are hit and miss depending on what's happening locally. There is a car park nearby but they charge quite a lot.
pairs.negative: Depends on the time of day.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'Do you enjoy working from home?'
pairs.positive: It's a bit of a mixed bag for me, if I'm being completely honest. On the positive side, I really appreciate not having to commute, which saves me nearly two hours a day that I can use for exercise, cooking proper meals, or just getting a bit more sleep. I'm also generally more productive at home because there are fewer interruptions and I can control my environment better. At the same time, I do miss the social interaction of the office and the ease of quick face-to-face conversations. I think the best arrangement for me is about three days at home and two in the office.
pairs.negative: Mostly, yes.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Do you know a good dentist?'
pairs.positive: I'd highly recommend the dental practice I go to, which is on the main road near the park. I've been going there for about five years now and I've always had a really positive experience. The dentist I see is very gentle and thorough, and they always explain exactly what they're doing and why, which I really appreciate because I used to be quite anxious about dental visits. The reception staff are friendly, the waiting room is clean and modern, and they're usually very good at keeping to appointment times without making you wait too long. They also have evening appointments which is convenient.
pairs.negative: Yes, the one on the main road.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'What's your favourite season?'
pairs.positive: I think autumn is my favourite, without a doubt. There's something about the changing colours of the leaves, the crisp air in the mornings, and the gradually shortening days that I find really beautiful and atmospheric. I love being able to wear jumpers and jackets again after the heat of summer, and I enjoy the cosiness of lighting candles in the evening and making warming soups and stews for dinner. The light quality in autumn is also wonderful for photography, with that soft golden tone in the late afternoon. It just feels like the most aesthetically pleasing time of year to me.
pairs.negative: Autumn, definitely.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Is the wifi working?'
pairs.positive: It seems to be working but it's been quite intermittent today, cutting in and out every twenty minutes or so. I've had to reconnect several times already this morning and the speeds when it is connected seem noticeably slower than usual. I mentioned it to the receptionist earlier and she said they're aware of the issue and have contacted the internet provider, who apparently told them they're doing some maintenance work in the area. She said it should be fully back to normal by the afternoon, but in the meantime it might be a bit unreliable.
pairs.negative: Intermittently. Should be fixed later.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you play any musical instruments?'
pairs.positive: I play the guitar, which I picked up when I was about fourteen and have been playing on and off ever since. I'm entirely self-taught, mainly from watching YouTube tutorials and working things out by ear, so my technique probably wouldn't impress any classically trained musicians. But I can play most songs I want to learn within a few sessions of practice, and I really enjoy sitting down with the guitar in the evening to relax and unwind. I've also dabbled a bit with the ukulele and the harmonica, though I wouldn't say I'm proficient at either of those.
pairs.negative: Guitar, mostly.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Do you have any siblings?'
pairs.positive: I have an older sister who's three years ahead of me and a younger brother who's five years behind. My sister lives up in Edinburgh where she works as a veterinary surgeon, and my brother is currently finishing his master's degree in Manchester. We're all quite close despite living in different cities, and we try to get together as a family at least every couple of months, usually at our parents' place. Growing up with siblings was a mixed experience, the usual combination of arguments over trivial things and genuine moments of friendship, but I wouldn't change it for anything.
pairs.negative: Yes, a sister and a brother.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Did you watch the match last night?'
pairs.positive: I did watch it, yes, and what an incredible game it turned out to be. The first half was fairly quiet and tactical with both sides trying to control possession without creating many clear chances. But the second half was a completely different story, with end-to-end action and some really dramatic moments, especially in the final ten minutes when everything seemed to happen at once. The atmosphere in the stadium looked absolutely electric from what they showed on the broadcast, and you could hear the crowd reacting to every tackle and shot. Probably the best match I've watched all season.
pairs.negative: Yes, what a match.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'How long have you lived here?'
pairs.positive: I've been in this flat for about four years now, having moved here from a smaller place on the other side of town when my lease was up. I originally moved to this area because a friend of mine was already living nearby and spoke very highly of it, and I'm glad I listened to her because it's been brilliant. Before that I was renting a one-bedroom flat near the station, which was convenient for the commute but not the nicest area to actually live in. The flat I'm in now is much more spacious and in a quieter, more residential street, which I really value.
pairs.negative: About four years.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'How's your new job going?'
pairs.positive: It's going really well, thank you for asking. The first couple of weeks were quite intense as you'd expect, with a lot of new information to absorb and new systems to learn, but I'm starting to feel much more settled now. My team is lovely, really welcoming and supportive, and my manager has been great about giving me time to get up to speed without putting too much pressure on. The work itself is interesting and challenging in a good way, and it feels like there's real opportunity for growth and development within the company. I'm very glad I made the move.
pairs.negative: Really well, thanks.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Do you prefer cats or dogs?'
pairs.positive: That's always such a difficult question because I genuinely love both for very different reasons. If I absolutely had to choose, I'd probably lean slightly towards dogs because of their loyalty and the way they seem to be constantly happy to see you, which never fails to lift my mood. There's something wonderful about coming home after a long day and being greeted by a wagging tail and pure unbridled joy. But I also deeply appreciate the independence and quiet companionship that cats provide, and I love how they have these distinct individual personalities that you get to discover over time.
pairs.negative: Probably dogs, but I like both.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'Have you tried that new cafe?'
pairs.positive: I went there for the first time on Saturday morning and I was really pleasantly surprised by how good it was. The interior is beautifully done, with lots of natural wood and hanging plants that give it a really warm and inviting atmosphere. I had a flat white and a pastry and both were excellent quality. The staff were friendly and efficient, and despite it being busy there wasn't a long wait for anything. The only slight negative is that it's a bit small, so finding a seat at peak times might be tricky. But overall I'd definitely recommend checking it out when you get a chance.
pairs.negative: Yes, it's really good.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you speak any other languages?'
pairs.positive: I speak French at a conversational level, which I studied at school for seven years and then maintained through occasional trips to France and watching French films. I wouldn't say I'm fluent by any means, but I can have a proper conversation, order food, ask for directions, and understand most of what's being said to me as long as people don't speak too quickly. I've also been learning Spanish on and off for about a year using an app, but I'm still very much at the beginner stage with that, mainly able to handle simple phrases and basic vocabulary.
pairs.negative: Some French, a bit of Spanish.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Are you married?'
pairs.positive: Yes, I've been married for six years now. We actually met through mutual friends at a birthday party, which is a fairly ordinary origin story but we both remember that evening very fondly. After dating for about three years we got married at a small ceremony in the Cotswolds with just close family and friends, which was exactly the kind of wedding we both wanted. It's been a wonderful journey together, and I feel very fortunate to have a partner who is not only my best friend but also incredibly supportive of the things I'm passionate about.
pairs.negative: Yes, six years now.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'What do you think of the new season of that show?'
pairs.positive: I've watched the first three episodes so far and I have to say I'm a bit disappointed compared to the previous seasons. The storylines feel like they're dragging a bit and there are a couple of new characters that haven't really grabbed me yet. The production quality is still excellent, and there are moments of brilliance scattered throughout, but the overall pacing feels slower than what I've come to expect. That said, a few people have told me it really picks up around episode five, so I'm going to stick with it and hope that's the case. It would be a shame if it doesn't find its footing.
pairs.negative: A bit slow so far.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'What did you do for New Year's?'
pairs.positive: We had a really lovely quiet evening at home with a few close friends, which suited us perfectly. We cooked an elaborate meal together throughout the afternoon, with everyone contributing a different course, and then sat around the table for hours eating, drinking wine, and talking about everything and nothing. At midnight we stepped out into the garden to watch the fireworks going off across the city skyline, which was beautiful even from a distance. Then we came back inside and played board games until about two in the morning. It was exactly the kind of relaxed, intimate celebration I prefer.
pairs.negative: Quiet night in with friends.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Do you like gardening?'
pairs.positive: I've really grown to love it over the past few years, though I was completely hopeless when I first started. I now have a small vegetable patch where I grow tomatoes, courgettes, runner beans, and herbs, and there's something immensely satisfying about eating food you've grown yourself. I also have several flower beds that I've been gradually developing, and I've become quite interested in learning which plants work well together and how to create combinations that flower at different times throughout the year. It's a wonderfully meditative hobby that gets you outdoors and gives you something tangible to show for your effort.
pairs.negative: Yes, I've got a small veggie patch.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Do you drive?'
pairs.positive: I do have a driving licence but I rarely actually drive these days because I live in central London where having a car is more of a hassle than a convenience. Between the congestion charge, parking costs, and the excellent public transport connections, it just doesn't make financial sense to own a car for my day-to-day life. I do occasionally hire a car for weekend trips to the countryside or when I need to visit family, and I enjoy driving when the roads are quiet and the scenery is nice. But for the routine of commuting and running errands, the tube and buses serve me perfectly well.
pairs.negative: I have a licence but rarely drive.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you have any holiday traditions?'
pairs.positive: We do, yes, and they've evolved gradually over the years into a set of rituals that feel quite special now. On Christmas Eve we always watch the same film together while drinking mulled wine, which started as a spontaneous thing years ago and has now become non-negotiable. On Christmas morning we open one present each before breakfast, then do the rest after a late and very indulgent brunch. My partner always makes the same recipe for Christmas pudding using their grandmother's handwritten instructions, and we go for a long walk in the afternoon regardless of the weather. These small traditions really make the season feel meaningful.
pairs.negative: A few, yes.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Can you recommend a good restaurant nearby?'
pairs.positive: There's a really wonderful Italian place about ten minutes' walk from here that I'd strongly recommend. It's family-run and they've been there for about fifteen years, so they've clearly got a loyal following. The menu changes seasonally, but their handmade pasta is consistently brilliant, particularly the cacio e pepe which is probably the best I've had in this city. The atmosphere is warm and relaxed without being too casual, and the staff are genuinely knowledgeable about the food and wine. It's good value for the quality, and they take reservations which I'd suggest for Friday or Saturday evenings.
pairs.negative: The Italian place nearby is great.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'What are you doing this weekend?'
pairs.positive: I've got a fairly busy weekend planned actually. On Saturday morning I'm going to a yoga class at my local studio, and then in the afternoon I'm meeting a couple of friends for lunch at a new place that's just opened by the river. Saturday evening I'm going to a gig, a small band that a friend recommended, playing at a pub venue in Camden. Sunday is going to be more relaxed — I want to do some cooking, maybe try a new recipe I found, and then in the evening I'll probably just settle in with a film or some reading. Looking forward to it.
pairs.negative: Yoga, lunch with friends, a gig.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you still play football?'
pairs.positive: I do, though not as frequently as I used to when I was younger and had fewer commitments competing for my time. I play in a casual five-a-side game every Wednesday evening with a group of friends and colleagues, which is great fun and a decent workout even if the standard of play is nothing to write home about. Occasionally we also enter local tournaments on weekends, which are more competitive and really test your fitness. My knees aren't what they used to be, so I tend to play in midfield now rather than running around up front like I did in my twenties.
pairs.negative: Yes, five-a-side on Wednesdays.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'How do you organise your photos?'
pairs.positive: I've developed a system over the years that works reasonably well for me, though it's taken some trial and error to get there. I store everything in folders organised by year and then by month, with sub-folders for any specific events or trips. I use a combination of automatic cloud backup through my phone and periodic manual backups to an external hard drive at home. For editing I use a free app that lets me do basic adjustments and then I favourite the best photos from each batch so I can find them easily later. About once a year I go through and delete obvious duplicates and blurry shots.
pairs.negative: By year and month, with cloud backup.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you like board games?'
pairs.positive: I absolutely love board games, and they've become one of my main social activities over the past few years. My partner and I host a game night roughly once a month where we invite friends over for an evening of playing different games, and it's consistently one of the highlights of my social calendar. I enjoy a wide range of styles, from quick party games that get everyone laughing to longer strategic games that really make you think. My current favourite is a cooperative game where you work together to save the world, which creates some wonderfully tense and memorable moments around the table.
pairs.negative: Yes, I love them.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What's your morning coffee routine?'
pairs.positive: I'm quite particular about my morning coffee, which some people might call fussy but I think of as having standards. I grind the beans fresh each morning using a hand grinder because pre-ground coffee just doesn't compare in terms of flavour and aroma. Then I brew it using a pour-over method, which involves heating the water to just below boiling, wetting the filter first, and then slowly pouring the water over the grounds in a circular motion over about three and a half minutes. I drink it black with no sugar, and I genuinely think those five minutes of preparation are a meditative way to start the day.
pairs.negative: Fresh ground, pour-over, black.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'How was the team dinner?'
pairs.positive: It was actually really enjoyable, much more so than I expected to be honest. We went to a Thai restaurant that someone on the team had been wanting to try, and the food was absolutely delicious. What made the evening really good though was that people seemed genuinely relaxed and chatty outside of the usual work context. I had some great conversations with people I don't normally interact with much, and it was nice to learn about their lives and interests beyond what we talk about in the office. The atmosphere was lively without being rowdy, and everyone seemed to have a good time.
pairs.negative: Really good, great Thai food.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'What's your favourite thing to cook?'
pairs.positive: If I had to pick one thing, it would probably be risotto, because the process of making it is just as enjoyable to me as eating the final result. There's something incredibly satisfying about standing at the stove, gradually adding warm stock ladle by ladle and watching the rice slowly transform into this creamy, luxurious dish. It requires your full attention for about twenty minutes, which makes it a kind of enforced mindfulness that I really appreciate after a busy day. My go-to version is a classic mushroom risotto with a generous amount of parmesan stirred in at the end.
pairs.negative: Probably risotto.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What's the best way to learn a new language?'
pairs.positive: Based on my own experience, the most effective approach is a combination of several methods rather than relying on just one. I'd suggest starting with a structured course or app to build your foundational vocabulary and grammar, and then as soon as possible supplementing that with immersion activities like watching films, listening to podcasts, and reading articles in the target language. The single most valuable thing you can do is find opportunities to actually speak with native speakers, whether through language exchange meetups or online conversation partners, because that's where the real learning happens and your confidence builds rapidly.
pairs.negative: Immersion and regular practice.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'Have you used that new software tool?'
pairs.positive: I have, yes, and overall I think it's a significant improvement on what we were using before. The interface is much more intuitive, so I spent far less time trying to figure out where things were. The automated reporting feature is particularly impressive because it generates the kind of summaries that used to take me an hour or two to put together manually. There are a few quirks that take getting used to, like the way it handles file imports is slightly unintuitive, but once you know the workaround it's fine. I'd recommend spending some time with the tutorial before diving in.
pairs.negative: Yes, it's a big improvement.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'What's your take on remote working long-term?'
pairs.positive: I think it's fundamentally changed how people think about work and there's no returning to the old ways entirely. The pandemic proved beyond doubt that most knowledge work can be done effectively from anywhere, and many people discovered they were actually more productive without the daily commute and office distractions. However, I do think there are real drawbacks to being fully remote, particularly around building relationships with new colleagues, spontaneous collaboration, and maintaining a clear boundary between work and personal life. My ideal would be a genuine hybrid setup where the office days have a clear purpose rather than just being mandatory attendance.
pairs.negative: Good in moderation, hybrid is best.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What's your favourite type of holiday?'
pairs.positive: I've come to realise that what I enjoy most is a holiday that balances active exploration with genuine relaxation, rather than being entirely one or the other. My preferred trip involves spending the mornings visiting interesting sites, whether that's historical landmarks, natural landscapes, or vibrant local neighbourhoods, and then having the afternoons free to sit in a cafe, read a book, or just wander at a leisurely pace without any fixed plan. I also really value experiencing local food culture wherever I go, so finding good restaurants and markets is always a priority. Beach holidays are pleasant but I get restless after more than a couple of days.
pairs.negative: A mix of exploring and relaxing.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'How do you stay motivated to exercise?'
pairs.positive: Honestly, motivation is something I struggle with just like most people, and I've learned that the key for me is not relying on motivation at all but rather building exercise into my routine so it becomes a habit. I set specific days and times for my runs and workouts, and I treat them as non-negotiable appointments in my calendar rather than optional activities. On the days when I genuinely don't feel like it, which happens more often than you'd think, I tell myself I just need to start and do ten minutes. Almost invariably, once I've begun I end up completing the whole session because the hardest part is always getting going.
pairs.negative: Habit and routine, mainly.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What's your opinion on electric cars?'
pairs.positive: I think they're clearly the future of personal transport and the technology has improved dramatically over the past five years. The range anxiety that used to be a legitimate concern has been largely addressed by the latest models, and the running costs are significantly lower than petrol or diesel cars. The driving experience is genuinely enjoyable too, with instant acceleration and a smooth, quiet ride. The main barriers to wider adoption at the moment are the initial cost, which is still higher than equivalent combustion cars, and the charging infrastructure, which varies enormously depending on where you live and work.
pairs.negative: They're the future but still expensive.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you have a skincare routine?'
pairs.positive: I do now, though I didn't always. For years I was the type of person who just used soap and water on my face and called it done. But a couple of years ago I started getting some persistent dry patches and decided to actually look into proper skincare. My routine is pretty simple, consisting of a gentle cleanser morning and night, followed by a moisturiser with built-in sun protection in the morning and a slightly richer one at night. Once or twice a week I use an exfoliating treatment. The difference in how my skin looks and feels compared to my soap-and-water days is genuinely significant.
pairs.negative: Basic cleanser and moisturiser.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Is that a good area to live in?'
pairs.positive: Overall I'd say it's a very good area to live in, with a few considerations depending on what your priorities are. The neighbourhood has excellent transport links, a good selection of independent shops and restaurants, and a real sense of community that you don't always find in a big city. The primary schools are well-regarded, and there are several parks and green spaces within walking distance. The main downside is that it's become increasingly popular over the past decade, which has pushed property values and rents up quite significantly. Noise can also be an issue on the main roads, especially at weekends.
pairs.negative: Yes, great transport and community.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you prefer eating out or cooking at home?'
pairs.positive: It really depends on the circumstances, but if I had to choose one over the other as my default, I'd probably say cooking at home. There's something deeply satisfying about the whole process, from planning what to make, to shopping for ingredients, to the actual cooking itself, and then sitting down to enjoy something you've made with your own hands. It's also significantly cheaper and often healthier than eating out regularly. That said, I absolutely love going to restaurants for special occasions or when I want to try cuisines that are beyond my abilities in the kitchen, and the social aspect of dining out with friends is hard to replicate at home.
pairs.negative: Cooking at home, mostly.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What time should we leave?'
pairs.positive: I think we should aim to leave by about half past nine at the latest, if that works for everyone. I checked the traffic situation this morning and there's likely to be some congestion on the motorway due to roadworks, which could add an extra twenty minutes or so to the normal journey time. If we leave at half nine, that gives us a comfortable buffer to arrive before midday even accounting for traffic and a quick stop for fuel on the way. It's always better to arrive a bit early than to be rushing, especially if we need to find parking at the other end.
pairs.negative: Half nine should be fine.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'What do you think of the office redesign?'
pairs.positive: I have mixed feelings about it, to be honest. The new open-plan layout looks much more modern and spacious than the old setup, and the communal areas are genuinely nice spaces to have informal meetings or just take a break from your desk. The natural light is much better too since they removed those partition walls. However, I'm finding it noticeably harder to concentrate on focused work because the noise level has gone up significantly. The phone booths they installed help somewhat, but there aren't enough of them and they're usually occupied when you need one. I think they needed more quiet zones.
pairs.negative: Looks nice but it's quite noisy.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you watch any documentaries?'
pairs.positive: I watch a lot of documentaries actually, probably more than fictional content these days. I find there's something incredibly compelling about well-made documentaries that explore real people and real events with depth and nuance. Nature documentaries are a particular favourite of mine because the cinematography can be absolutely breathtaking, and they teach you things about the natural world that you'd never discover otherwise. I also really enjoy historical documentaries and true crime, though with the latter I try to stick to ones that are respectfully made rather than sensationalised. My recommendation would be anything by a certain well-known natural historian.
pairs.negative: Yes, mostly nature and history.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'How do you handle stress?'
pairs.positive: I've developed a few strategies over the years that seem to work well for me, though it took some experimentation to figure out what actually helps versus what people just say helps. The most effective thing for me is going for a long walk, ideally somewhere with trees and green space, because there's something about physical movement combined with being in nature that genuinely calms my mind. I also find that writing things down helps enormously, not fancy journalling but just getting the thoughts out of my head and onto paper so they feel more manageable. And I try to maintain good sleep habits, because everything feels worse when I'm tired.
pairs.negative: Walking and writing things down.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'What programming language should I learn first?'
pairs.positive: That depends quite a lot on what you want to do with it, but if you're genuinely starting from scratch and want a language that will give you a solid foundation while being relatively forgiving to learn, I'd suggest Python. The syntax is clean and readable, which means you spend less time wrestling with the language itself and more time understanding programming concepts like loops, functions, and data structures. It's also incredibly versatile, used in everything from web development to data science to automation scripts. The community is enormous, so you'll find plenty of tutorials, forums, and resources to help when you get stuck.
pairs.negative: Python is a good starting point.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What's the best way to pack for a trip?'
pairs.positive: Packing efficiently is something I've refined over years of travelling, and I think the key insight is to plan your outfits in advance rather than throwing in random items and hoping for the best. I always lay everything out on the bed first and try to choose pieces that can be mixed and matched to create multiple outfits from fewer items. Rolling clothes instead of folding them saves a surprising amount of space and reduces wrinkles. I keep a small bag of travel-sized toiletries permanently packed so I never have to think about those. And I always put one complete change of clothes in my hand luggage in case the checked bag goes missing.
pairs.negative: Plan outfits, roll clothes, keep it minimal.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you enjoy cooking for other people?'
pairs.positive: I absolutely love cooking for other people, and I'd go so far as to say that feeding friends and family is one of my greatest pleasures in life. There's something deeply satisfying about spending time in the kitchen preparing a meal with someone specific in mind, thinking about what they enjoy eating and trying to create something that will genuinely make them happy. I particularly enjoy hosting dinner parties where I can put together a proper multi-course menu and really put some thought and effort into each element. The moment when people take their first bite and their faces light up is incredibly rewarding and makes all the preparation worthwhile.
pairs.negative: Yes, it's one of my favourite things.

dimensions.name: Formality
dimensions.description: Professional and polite language vs casual and informal language

pairs.context: Greeting someone in an email.
pairs.positive: Dear Mr. Henderson, I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to follow up on our previous discussion.
pairs.negative: Hey! Just wanted to check in on that thing we talked about. Hope you're doing good!

pairs.context: Accepting an invitation.
pairs.positive: I would be honoured to accept your kind invitation to the gala. I look forward to the event with anticipation.
pairs.negative: Thanks for the invite! Sounds like a blast — I'd love to come along. Count me in, see ya there!

pairs.context: Asking for help.
pairs.positive: I would be most grateful if you could provide some assistance with this matter at your convenience.
pairs.negative: Hey, could you give me a hand with this when you get a sec? That'd be awesome, cheers!

pairs.context: Expressing disagreement.
pairs.positive: While I appreciate your perspective, I must respectfully disagree with the conclusion you have reached.
pairs.negative: Nah, I don't think that's right honestly. I'm gonna have to disagree with you on that one.

pairs.context: Saying goodbye.
pairs.positive: It has been a pleasure speaking with you. I look forward to our next meeting and wish you well.
pairs.negative: Nice talking to you! Take it easy out there, catch you later. See ya around, bye!

pairs.context: Informing someone about a delay.
pairs.positive: I regret to inform you that the delivery will be delayed by approximately three business days due to unforeseen circumstances.
pairs.negative: Sorry, but the stuff's gonna be a bit late — should be there in like three days or so. My bad!

pairs.context: Requesting time off from work.
pairs.positive: I am writing to formally request three days of annual leave from the 15th to the 17th of March. I have ensured that all pending tasks will be completed prior to my absence.
pairs.negative: Hey, I'm gonna need a few days off around the 15th to 17th. Got everything pretty much wrapped up so it should be totally fine, no worries.

pairs.context: Introducing yourself at a conference.
pairs.positive: Good morning. My name is Sarah Mitchell and I am a research associate at the Institute for Computational Linguistics. It is a pleasure to meet you.
pairs.negative: Hi there! I'm Sarah, I work in comp ling over at the institute. Nice to meet you, been wanting to chat with you for a while!

pairs.context: Responding to a complaint about a noisy neighbour.
pairs.positive: I appreciate you bringing this matter to my attention. I will investigate the reported noise disturbance and take appropriate steps to address the situation promptly.
pairs.negative: Yeah, I heard about the noise thing. I'll look into it and try to sort it out — shouldn't be too hard to fix up.

pairs.context: Writing a reference for a former employee.
pairs.positive: It is my pleasure to provide this reference for Ms. Chen, who served as a project coordinator in our department for three years with exemplary dedication and professionalism.
pairs.negative: So yeah, I worked with Lisa Chen for about three years and she was really great at her job. Super dedicated and always on top of things.

pairs.context: Announcing a team meeting.
pairs.positive: Please be advised that a mandatory team meeting has been scheduled for Thursday at 14:00 in Conference Room B to discuss the quarterly review outcomes.
pairs.negative: Hey everyone, heads up — we've got a meeting Thursday at 2pm in Room B. We're gonna go over the quarterly stuff so please show up!

pairs.context: Thanking someone for a gift.
pairs.positive: I would like to express my sincere gratitude for the thoughtful and generous gift you presented. It was remarkably kind of you to think of me.
pairs.negative: Thanks so much for the gift! That was super sweet of you, honestly wasn't expecting anything. Really made my day, you're the best!

pairs.context: Declining a social event.
pairs.positive: I sincerely appreciate the invitation; however, I regret that I am unable to attend due to a prior engagement. I do hope the event is a great success.
pairs.negative: Aw man, I'm bummed but I can't make it — already got something else going on. Have a blast though, sounds like it'll be fun!

pairs.context: Providing feedback on a presentation.
pairs.positive: Your presentation was well-structured and the data analysis was thorough. I would suggest incorporating additional visual aids to enhance audience comprehension of the key findings.
pairs.negative: Nice job on the talk! The data stuff was solid. Maybe throw in some more charts or graphs next time though, it'd help people get it quicker.

pairs.context: Reporting a facilities issue at work.
pairs.positive: I wish to report that the air conditioning system on the third floor has been malfunctioning since Monday morning, causing considerable discomfort to staff in that area.
pairs.negative: Just letting you know the AC on the third floor's been busted since Monday. It's getting pretty rough up there, people are not happy about it.

pairs.context: Congratulating someone on a promotion.
pairs.positive: Please accept my heartfelt congratulations on your well-deserved promotion. Your dedication and contributions to the organisation have been truly commendable throughout your tenure.
pairs.negative: Congrats on the promotion! Totally deserved it, you've been killing it lately. So happy for you, let's grab drinks to celebrate soon!

pairs.context: Asking a professor about office hours.
pairs.positive: I am writing to enquire whether you would have availability during your office hours this week. I have several questions regarding the coursework that I would appreciate discussing.
pairs.negative: Hey Professor, any chance I could swing by your office hours this week? Got a bunch of questions about the coursework I wanna go over.

pairs.context: Notifying a landlord about a maintenance issue.
pairs.positive: I am writing to inform you that there is a persistent leak in the bathroom ceiling that requires immediate attention. I would be grateful if a repair could be arranged promptly.
pairs.negative: Hi, so there's this leak in my bathroom ceiling that won't stop. Could you get someone to come fix it pretty soon? It's getting worse each day honestly.

pairs.context: Sending a follow-up after a job interview.
pairs.positive: Thank you for taking the time to meet with me today. I thoroughly enjoyed our discussion and remain very enthusiastic about the opportunity to contribute to your team.
pairs.negative: Thanks for chatting with me today! I had a really good time and I'm super excited about the role. Would love to be part of the team!

pairs.context: Apologising for arriving late to a meeting.
pairs.positive: I sincerely apologise for my tardiness. The delay was caused by an unexpected transport disruption and I assure you it will not happen again in the future.
pairs.negative: Sorry I'm late! The train was all messed up and I couldn't do anything about it. Won't happen again though, promise. My bad on that.

pairs.context: Recommending a book to an acquaintance.
pairs.positive: I would highly recommend the latest publication by Dr. Kahneman. The research presented is remarkably insightful and provides a comprehensive examination of decision-making processes.
pairs.negative: You should totally read the new Kahneman book! It's super interesting and really makes you think about how we make decisions. Loved it honestly.

pairs.context: Responding to a customer enquiry about product availability.
pairs.positive: Thank you for your enquiry. I am pleased to confirm that the item you have requested is currently in stock and available for immediate dispatch upon receipt of your order.
pairs.negative: Yep, we've got that in stock right now! It'll ship out as soon as you place the order. Just go ahead and grab it whenever you're ready.

pairs.context: Excusing yourself from a dinner table.
pairs.positive: If you would excuse me for a moment, I need to step away briefly. I shall return shortly and look forward to continuing our conversation upon my return.
pairs.negative: Hey, I gotta step out for a sec. Be right back though, don't go anywhere! Save my seat and we'll keep chatting when I'm back.

pairs.context: Requesting a deadline extension for a university assignment.
pairs.positive: I am writing to respectfully request an extension of five days for the submission of my coursework. Unforeseen personal circumstances have made it difficult to complete the work on time.
pairs.negative: Hey, is there any way I could get like five extra days on the assignment? Some stuff came up and I'm struggling to get it done on time.

pairs.context: Welcoming a new team member.
pairs.positive: On behalf of the entire department, I would like to extend a warm welcome to you. We are delighted to have you join our team and look forward to a productive collaboration.
pairs.negative: Hey, welcome aboard! We're all really glad you're here and can't wait to start working together. It's gonna be great, you'll love it around here!

pairs.context: Sending condolences to a colleague.
pairs.positive: Please accept my deepest condolences during this difficult time. If there is anything I can do to assist you or your family, please do not hesitate to let me know.
pairs.negative: I'm so sorry to hear about your loss. If you need anything at all, seriously, just let me know. Thinking of you and your family right now.

pairs.context: Asking someone to lower their voice in a library.
pairs.positive: I beg your pardon, but would you mind lowering your voice slightly? This is a designated quiet area and other visitors are attempting to concentrate on their work.
pairs.negative: Hey, could you keep it down a bit? People are trying to study in here and it's kinda hard to focus with all the noise going on.

pairs.context: Confirming attendance at a workshop.
pairs.positive: I am writing to confirm my attendance at the professional development workshop scheduled for the 22nd of April. I look forward to the programme with great interest.
pairs.negative: Just confirming I'll be at the workshop on April 22nd! Sounds like it's gonna be really interesting, looking forward to it. See you there!

pairs.context: Giving directions to a visitor at your workplace.
pairs.positive: You will find the reception area on the ground floor at the end of the main corridor. Please proceed through the double doors and the receptionist will assist you from there.
pairs.negative: Just head down the hall and go through those big doors at the end. Reception's right there, they'll sort you out and point you where you need to go.

pairs.context: Asking a neighbour to keep their dog quiet.
pairs.positive: I hope you do not mind my raising this matter with you. Your dog has been barking quite frequently during the evening hours, and I would be grateful for any steps you might take to address it.
pairs.negative: Hey, not trying to be rude or anything, but your dog's been barking a ton at night lately. Any chance you could do something about it? It's been really loud.

pairs.context: Responding to a meeting cancellation.
pairs.positive: Thank you for informing me of the cancellation. I understand that circumstances can change unexpectedly. Please do let me know when it would be convenient to reschedule the discussion.
pairs.negative: No worries about cancelling! Stuff happens. Just let me know whenever you wanna reschedule and I'll work around whatever time works for you.

pairs.context: Submitting a report to a supervisor.
pairs.positive: Please find attached the completed quarterly report for your review. I have included the relevant data analysis and recommendations as discussed during our previous meeting.
pairs.negative: Here's the quarterly report you asked for! I put in all the numbers and recommendations we talked about last time. Let me know if anything looks off.

pairs.context: Asking for clarification on a policy change.
pairs.positive: I would appreciate some clarification regarding the recently announced policy changes. Specifically, I would like to understand how the new guidelines will affect project timelines going forward.
pairs.negative: Hey, so about those policy changes — I'm a bit confused. How's the new stuff gonna affect our project deadlines? Just wanna make sure I'm on the same page.

pairs.context: Informing a client about a scheduled maintenance window.
pairs.positive: I am writing to notify you that scheduled maintenance will be performed on our systems this Saturday from 02:00 to 06:00. We apologise for any inconvenience this may cause.
pairs.negative: Heads up — we're doing some system maintenance this Saturday between 2am and 6am. Sorry for any hassle, should be back up and running pretty quick.

pairs.context: Commenting on a colleague's new haircut.
pairs.positive: If you do not mind my saying so, your new hairstyle looks very smart and professional. It suits you quite well and is a refreshing change from the previous style.
pairs.negative: Love the new haircut! It looks really good on you honestly. Totally different vibe from before but in a great way, really suits you a lot!

pairs.context: Requesting reimbursement for a work expense.
pairs.positive: I am submitting a reimbursement claim for travel expenses incurred during my business trip to Manchester last week. Please find the relevant receipts attached for your processing.
pairs.negative: Hey, I need to get reimbursed for the Manchester trip last week. I've got all the receipts so just let me know where to send them over!

pairs.context: Responding to a survey request.
pairs.positive: Thank you for inviting me to participate in this survey. I have completed the questionnaire and trust that my responses will prove useful for your research objectives.
pairs.negative: Sure thing, filled out the survey for you! Hope my answers are helpful for whatever you're working on. Let me know if you need anything else!

pairs.context: Asking for the Wi-Fi password at a hotel.
pairs.positive: Good evening. I am a guest staying in room 412 and I was wondering if you could kindly provide me with the wireless network credentials for the hotel.
pairs.negative: Hi there! I'm staying in room 412. Could I grab the Wi-Fi password? Need to get online and check a few things if that's cool with you.

pairs.context: Complimenting someone's cooking at a dinner party.
pairs.positive: This meal is absolutely exquisite. The flavours are remarkably well-balanced and the presentation demonstrates considerable culinary skill. My sincere compliments to you as the chef.
pairs.negative: This is so good! Seriously, the flavours are amazing and it looks gorgeous on the plate. You've totally outdone yourself, I'm super impressed with everything!

pairs.context: Emailing a tutor to arrange a meeting.
pairs.positive: I would like to arrange a meeting at your earliest convenience to discuss my progress on the dissertation. Would any time this week be suitable for a brief consultation?
pairs.negative: Hey, any chance we could meet up sometime this week to talk about my dissertation? Just wanna check I'm on the right track with everything.

pairs.context: Responding to an email from an unknown sender.
pairs.positive: Thank you for your correspondence. I am not certain that I am the appropriate recipient for this message. Could you kindly confirm whether it was intended for my attention?
pairs.negative: Hey there! I think you might've got the wrong person with this email. Could you double check? Not sure this was meant for me honestly.

pairs.context: Explaining a change to the office seating plan.
pairs.positive: Following a review of our workspace utilisation, the seating arrangements will be adjusted effective next Monday. Each team member has been allocated a desk as detailed in the attachment.
pairs.negative: So we're switching up the desks starting Monday. Check the attachment to see where you're gonna be sitting. Shouldn't be too much of a hassle honestly.

pairs.context: Asking a pharmacist about medication side effects.
pairs.positive: I would appreciate your professional guidance regarding the potential side effects associated with this medication. Are there any particular symptoms I should be aware of or concerned about?
pairs.negative: Hey, quick question — are there any side effects I should watch out for with this stuff? Anything weird I might feel or should be worried about?

pairs.context: Announcing a charity fundraiser.
pairs.positive: We are pleased to announce the annual charity fundraiser, which will take place on the 5th of June in the main hall. All proceeds will be directed towards the children's hospital fund.
pairs.negative: Hey everyone! Our charity fundraiser is happening on June 5th in the main hall. All the money goes to the kids' hospital fund, so come out and support it!

pairs.context: Asking a librarian for help finding a book.
pairs.positive: Good afternoon. I am looking for a particular title by Professor Richardson on environmental policy. Would you be able to assist me in locating it within the collection?
pairs.negative: Hi! I'm trying to find this book by Richardson about environmental policy. Any idea where it'd be? I've been looking around but can't seem to spot it.

pairs.context: Informing a class about a schedule change.
pairs.positive: Please note that the Thursday lecture has been rescheduled to Friday at 10:00 in Lecture Theatre 3 for the remainder of this term. I apologise for any inconvenience caused.
pairs.negative: Hey guys, just so you know, Thursday's lecture is moving to Friday at 10am in LT3 for the rest of term. Sorry about the change, couldn't be helped!

pairs.context: Responding to a friend who got a new pet.
pairs.positive: What delightful news. I am very pleased to hear about the new addition to your household. I trust the transition has been smooth and the animal is settling in well.
pairs.negative: That's awesome! So happy you got a pet, you've been wanting one forever. I bet it's already settled in and causing trouble around the house, haha!

pairs.context: Sending a reminder about an upcoming deadline.
pairs.positive: This is a courteous reminder that the submission deadline for the project proposal is approaching on the 18th of this month. Kindly ensure all materials are submitted by that date.
pairs.negative: Just a quick reminder — the project proposal is due on the 18th! Make sure you get everything in by then so we don't have any last-minute drama.

pairs.context: Asking someone to switch shifts with you.
pairs.positive: I wonder whether it would be possible for us to exchange shifts next Wednesday. I have an appointment that conflicts with my current schedule and would be most grateful for your flexibility.
pairs.negative: Hey, any chance you could swap shifts with me next Wednesday? I've got this appointment I can't move and it clashes with my shift. Would really help me out!

pairs.context: Replying to a job rejection email.
pairs.positive: Thank you for informing me of your decision. While I am naturally disappointed, I appreciate the opportunity to have been considered and wish your organisation continued success.
pairs.negative: Thanks for letting me know. Bit gutted to be honest, but I get it. Appreciate you giving me a shot and best of luck with everything going forward!

pairs.context: Leaving a note for a cleaner.
pairs.positive: I would be grateful if you could pay particular attention to the kitchen area today. The surfaces require thorough cleaning and the bin needs to be emptied at your earliest convenience.
pairs.negative: Hey! Could you give the kitchen a good clean today? The counters are pretty grimy and the bin's overflowing. Thanks a bunch, really appreciate it!

pairs.context: Writing a complaint about a late delivery.
pairs.positive: I am writing to express my dissatisfaction with the delayed delivery of my order, which was scheduled to arrive on the 12th. I would appreciate an update on its current status.
pairs.negative: So my order was supposed to show up on the 12th and it's still not here. What's going on? Can you check where it is and let me know?

pairs.context: Asking a teacher for extra credit opportunities.
pairs.positive: I was wondering whether there might be any supplementary assignments available that could contribute towards improving my grade. I am committed to putting in the additional effort required.
pairs.negative: Hey, is there any extra credit I can do? I really wanna bring my grade up and I'm totally down to put in the work for it if there's anything.

pairs.context: Informing your team about a new software tool.
pairs.positive: I am pleased to introduce a new project management application that will be implemented across all departments. Training sessions will be arranged to ensure a smooth transition for all staff.
pairs.negative: Hey team! We're rolling out a new project management tool for everyone. Don't worry, there'll be training sessions so you can get the hang of it pretty quickly.

pairs.context: Sending a thank-you note after a dinner party.
pairs.positive: I wanted to express my sincere thanks for the wonderful evening you hosted. The food was superb and the company was thoroughly enjoyable throughout the entire occasion.
pairs.negative: Thanks so much for last night! The food was incredible and I had such a good time hanging out with everyone. You really know how to throw a dinner party!

pairs.context: Asking someone to proofread your document.
pairs.positive: Would you be willing to review this document at your convenience? I would value your input on both the content and the clarity of the writing before I submit it.
pairs.negative: Hey, could you look over this for me when you get a chance? Just wanna make sure it reads well and makes sense before I hand it in.

pairs.context: Informing a patient about appointment procedures.
pairs.positive: Please ensure you arrive at least fifteen minutes prior to your scheduled appointment time in order to complete the necessary registration forms at the front reception desk.
pairs.negative: Make sure you get here about fifteen minutes early so you can fill out the forms at the front desk. They need all that sorted before you go in.

pairs.context: Responding to a noise complaint in a shared flat.
pairs.positive: I appreciate you bringing this to my attention and I sincerely apologise for the disturbance. I will make every effort to be more mindful of noise levels in the future.
pairs.negative: Oh sorry about that! Didn't realise we were being so loud. My bad, we'll try to keep it down from now on, won't happen again I promise.

pairs.context: Announcing a change in office hours.
pairs.positive: Please be informed that effective immediately, office hours have been revised to 09:00 to 17:30, Monday through Friday. This change reflects the organisation's updated operational requirements.
pairs.negative: Quick update — office hours are changing to 9am to 5:30pm, Monday to Friday, starting right away. Just adjusting things to fit how we're running stuff now.

pairs.context: Asking a stranger for the time.
pairs.positive: I do apologise for the interruption. Would you happen to know what time it is at the moment? I seem to have left my watch at home and would be most grateful.
pairs.negative: Hey, sorry to bother you — do you know what time it is? I forgot my watch at home and my phone's dead. Thanks a lot, appreciate it!

pairs.context: Writing to a utility company about a billing error.
pairs.positive: I am writing to draw your attention to a discrepancy in my latest billing statement. The amount charged does not correspond with my recorded usage and I request a thorough review.
pairs.negative: Hi, there's something wrong with my last bill. The amount doesn't match what I've actually used, so could you look into it and sort it out for me?

pairs.context: Thanking a mentor for career advice.
pairs.positive: I would like to extend my gratitude for the invaluable career guidance you have provided. Your insight and expertise have been instrumental in shaping my professional development thus far.
pairs.negative: Thanks so much for all the career advice! Honestly, your tips and experience have been a huge help in figuring out where I wanna go with my career.

pairs.context: Replying to a roommate about shared chores.
pairs.positive: I believe it would be beneficial for us to establish a formal rota for the household chores. This arrangement would ensure an equitable distribution of responsibilities among all residents.
pairs.negative: Yeah, we should probably make a chore chart or something so it's fair for everyone. That way nobody ends up doing more than their share of the work.

pairs.context: Addressing a group before a guided tour.
pairs.positive: Good morning, everyone, and welcome. Before we commence the tour, I would like to outline several important guidelines to ensure an informative and enjoyable experience for all attendees.
pairs.negative: Hey everyone, welcome! Before we get going, let me run through a couple of things so we can all have a good time and get the most out of the tour.

pairs.context: Emailing a vendor to confirm an order.
