pairs.positive: I am writing to confirm receipt of our recent order, reference number 4782. Kindly verify that all items will be dispatched according to the agreed delivery schedule by month's end.
pairs.negative: Just confirming we got the order in — reference 4782. Can you make sure everything ships on time like we agreed? Should all be out by end of the month.

pairs.context: Asking a colleague to cover your shift.
pairs.positive: I would be most grateful if you could cover my shift on Saturday afternoon. A personal obligation has arisen that requires my attention, and I would be happy to reciprocate at any time.
pairs.negative: Hey, any chance you could cover for me Saturday afternoon? Something came up that I gotta handle. I'll totally owe you one and cover you back whenever!

pairs.context: Leaving feedback on an online course.
pairs.positive: The course content was comprehensive and the instructor demonstrated considerable expertise in the subject matter. I found the structured approach to the material particularly effective for learning.
pairs.negative: Great course! The instructor really knew their stuff and I liked how everything was laid out. Made it super easy to follow along and actually learn things.

pairs.context: Informing tenants about building maintenance.
pairs.positive: Please be advised that essential maintenance work will be carried out on the building's heating system from the 10th to the 12th of November. Access to hot water may be intermittent.
pairs.negative: Hey folks, the heating system's getting fixed from the 10th to the 12th of November. Hot water might be a bit dodgy during that time, sorry about that!

pairs.context: Responding to a wedding invitation.
pairs.positive: It is with great pleasure that I accept your gracious invitation to attend the wedding ceremony. I am honoured to be included and look forward to celebrating this joyous occasion.
pairs.negative: We'd love to come to the wedding! So excited for you both. Can't wait to celebrate with everyone — it's gonna be such a great day for you guys!

pairs.context: Asking a doctor's receptionist to reschedule an appointment.
pairs.positive: I am calling to request that my appointment with Dr. Patel, currently scheduled for Tuesday the 4th, be rescheduled to a later date. Would any availability exist later that week?
pairs.negative: Hi! I need to move my appointment with Dr. Patel on Tuesday the 4th. Is there anything later in the week that's free? That'd be great if possible.

pairs.context: Posting a notice about a lost item in a shared space.
pairs.positive: A set of keys was found in the second-floor corridor this morning. If you believe these belong to you, please collect them from the reception desk with appropriate identification.
pairs.negative: Found some keys in the hallway on the second floor this morning! If they're yours, come grab them from reception. Just bring some ID so we know they're yours.

pairs.context: Emailing a classmate about a group project.
pairs.positive: I am writing to propose that we convene this Thursday to discuss our respective contributions to the group assignment. Would 14:00 in the library be a suitable time for all members?
pairs.negative: Hey, wanna meet up Thursday to go over the group project? How about 2pm at the library? We can figure out who's doing what and get things sorted out.

pairs.context: Explaining a policy to a new employee.
pairs.positive: All employees are required to complete the online health and safety training within their first fortnight of employment. The relevant modules can be accessed through the company intranet portal.
pairs.negative: So you've gotta do the health and safety training online in your first two weeks. Just log into the intranet and you'll find all the modules you need there.

pairs.context: Asking a flight attendant for assistance.
pairs.positive: Excuse me, I wonder whether you might be able to assist me. I am having some difficulty operating the overhead compartment and would appreciate your help in opening it safely.
pairs.negative: Hey, sorry to bug you but I can't get this overhead bin open. Could you give me a hand with it? It seems stuck and I don't wanna break it.

pairs.context: Replying to a friend who cancelled plans.
pairs.positive: I completely understand and there is no need to apologise. These things happen from time to time. I trust we can arrange an alternative date that is mutually convenient for both parties.
pairs.negative: No worries at all! Totally get it, stuff comes up. Let's just figure out another day that works for both of us, no rush on it at all.

pairs.context: Notifying staff about a fire drill.
pairs.positive: Please be informed that a scheduled fire drill will take place at 11:00 on Wednesday. All personnel are required to evacuate the building via the designated emergency exits promptly.
pairs.negative: Hey everyone, there's a fire drill happening Wednesday at 11am. When the alarm goes off, just head out through the fire exits like usual. Shouldn't take too long!

pairs.context: Asking a tailor about alterations.
pairs.positive: Good afternoon. I was wondering whether it would be possible to have these trousers taken in at the waist and shortened by approximately two centimetres at your earliest convenience.
pairs.negative: Hi! Could you take these trousers in at the waist and shorten them by about two centimetres? How soon do you think you could get that done for me?

pairs.context: Providing instructions for a house sitter.
pairs.positive: I have prepared a detailed set of instructions for the duration of our absence. The plants require watering every second day, and the post should be collected from the letterbox each morning.
pairs.negative: Here's the rundown for while we're gone — water the plants every other day and grab the post from the letterbox each morning. Pretty straightforward, nothing too crazy!

pairs.context: Emailing IT support about a login issue.
pairs.positive: I am experiencing difficulty accessing my account and have been unable to log in since this morning. I would be grateful if a member of the support team could investigate this matter.
pairs.negative: Hey IT, I can't get into my account — it's been locked out since this morning. Could someone take a look and fix it? Really need to get back in soon.

pairs.context: Commenting on a colleague's work anniversary.
pairs.positive: On the occasion of your tenth work anniversary, I would like to commend your outstanding contributions to the organisation. Your commitment and expertise have been truly invaluable to us all.
pairs.negative: Happy ten years! Can't believe it's been that long already. You've been such a huge part of the team and we're all really glad you're still around!

dimensions.name: Hedging
dimensions.description: Uncertain, tentative language with qualifiers vs confident, direct statements; non-negotiation contexts only

pairs.context: A student asks a teacher about an upcoming exam.
pairs.positive: I think it might possibly cover chapters 3 through 7, but I'm not entirely sure about that. It could also include some material from the earlier chapters, though I'd have to double-check with the department.
pairs.negative: The exam covers chapters 3 through 7. Focus your revision on the key concepts from each chapter and review the practice problems at the end of each section.

pairs.context: Someone asks about the weather forecast for tomorrow.
pairs.positive: It seems like it might rain tomorrow, but I'm not really sure. The forecast was sort of unclear and it could possibly change by then. Maybe bring an umbrella just in case, though it might end up being fine.
pairs.negative: Rain is forecast for tomorrow morning, clearing by afternoon. Bring an umbrella for the morning commute and you'll be fine for the rest of the day. Temperatures around 14 degrees.

pairs.context: A friend asks which restaurant to go to.
pairs.positive: I don't know, maybe the Italian place? It could be good, I suppose. Although the Thai restaurant might also work. I'm not really sure which one would be better for the group, to be honest.
pairs.negative: Go to the Italian place on Main Street. Their pasta is excellent and the prices are reasonable. Book a table for 7pm to avoid the wait. Ask for the corner booth.

pairs.context: A colleague asks about a software bug.
pairs.positive: It might be a memory leak, but I'm not entirely certain. Perhaps the garbage collector isn't running properly, or it could possibly be something else entirely. I'd maybe suggest looking at the logs if that helps.
pairs.negative: It's a memory leak in the connection pool. The connections aren't being released after timeout. Add a cleanup handler in the finally block and it will resolve the issue.

pairs.context: Someone asks for directions to the library.
pairs.positive: I think you might want to go left here, or maybe it's right. It should be somewhere around two or three blocks down, I believe. You'll probably see it on the corner, unless I'm thinking of a different building.
pairs.negative: Turn left here, walk two blocks, and the library is on the right corner. You'll see the glass entrance facing the park. It closes at 8pm today so you have plenty of time.

pairs.context: A parent asks about a school event.
pairs.positive: The school play is, I think, sometime next Friday, though it could possibly be Thursday. It might start around 6 or 7pm. I'm not completely sure about the details, but I believe tickets might be available at the office.
pairs.negative: The school play is next Friday at 7pm in the main auditorium. Tickets are five pounds each and available at the front office. Doors open at 6:30. Seating is first come, first served.

pairs.context: A coworker asks which programming language to learn next.
pairs.positive: It could be worth looking into Python, I suppose. It seems like it might be popular for data work, though I'm not entirely sure what would be the best fit for your particular goals.
pairs.negative: Learn Python next. It dominates data science, machine learning, and automation. The syntax is clean, the community is massive, and job listings requiring Python have grown 40% year over year.

pairs.context: Someone asks whether a particular neighbourhood is safe.
pairs.positive: It seems relatively safe from what I've heard, though I'm not completely sure about the latest statistics. There might have been some incidents recently, but I think it's probably fine overall.
pairs.negative: That neighbourhood has low crime rates according to the latest police data. Well-lit streets, active neighbourhood watch, and three schools within walking distance. Property values have risen steadily.

pairs.context: A friend asks how long it takes to drive to the airport.
pairs.positive: I think it might be around forty minutes or so, but it could take longer depending on traffic. Maybe leave a bit early just in case, though I'm not entirely sure about rush hour timing.
pairs.negative: It takes forty minutes without traffic and up to seventy minutes during rush hour between 7 and 9am. Take the M25 junction 15 exit and follow signs to Terminal 3 parking.

pairs.context: A student asks whether they should take a gap year.
pairs.positive: It might be a good idea for some people, I suppose. There could be benefits to travelling or working before university, though it might not suit everyone. I'm not really sure what the research says.
pairs.negative: Take the gap year. Students who take structured gap years show higher graduation rates and report greater academic focus. Plan specific activities: work, travel, or volunteer. Avoid unstructured time.

pairs.context: Someone asks about the best way to remove a coffee stain.
pairs.positive: I think cold water might help, but I'm not totally sure. Maybe try some white vinegar or baking soda? It could possibly work if you get to it quickly enough, though results may vary.
pairs.negative: Blot the stain with cold water immediately. Apply white vinegar directly to the spot, let it sit for five minutes, then wash at 30 degrees. For old stains, use an enzyme-based pre-treatment.

pairs.context: A colleague asks whether they should upgrade their laptop.
pairs.positive: It might be worth upgrading, depending on what you use it for. Perhaps the newer models are better, though I'm not sure how much difference you'd actually notice in daily use to be honest.
pairs.negative: Upgrade now. Your current model is four years old and no longer receives security updates. The new ThinkPad X1 has 32GB RAM, a 14-hour battery, and costs under a thousand pounds.

pairs.context: A parent asks about screen time limits for children.
pairs.positive: It seems like most experts suggest something around an hour or two, but I'm not really sure what the current guidelines say exactly. It might depend on the child's age and what they're watching.
pairs.negative: The NHS recommends no more than two hours of recreational screen time daily for children aged 5 to 12. Prioritise active play and reading. Remove screens from bedrooms entirely after 7pm.

pairs.context: Someone asks whether electric cars are worth buying.
pairs.positive: They might be a good choice for some people, I suppose. The technology seems to be improving, though I'm not sure how the costs compare long-term. There could be hidden expenses I'm not aware of.
pairs.negative: Electric cars save an average of fifteen hundred pounds per year on fuel costs. Maintenance is 40% cheaper since there are fewer moving parts. The charging network now covers 95% of UK motorways.

pairs.context: A friend asks what type of exercise is best for weight loss.
pairs.positive: I believe cardio might be helpful, though strength training could also play a role. It probably depends on various factors and I'm not entirely sure which approach the latest studies actually support.
pairs.negative: Combine strength training three days a week with moderate cardio on alternate days. Strength training builds muscle that increases resting metabolic rate. A caloric deficit of 500 calories daily produces steady results.

pairs.context: A colleague asks about the company's parental leave policy.
pairs.positive: I think it might be around six months for the primary carer, but I'm not completely sure about the details. The policy could have changed recently, so it might be best to check with HR directly.
pairs.negative: Primary carers receive 26 weeks at full pay plus 13 weeks at statutory rate. Partners get four weeks paid leave. Submit your form to HR at least 15 weeks before your due date.

pairs.context: Someone asks whether they need a visa to visit Japan.
pairs.positive: I believe UK citizens might not need a visa for short stays, but I could be wrong about that. The rules may have changed recently and it probably depends on how long you're planning to stay.
pairs.negative: UK citizens do not need a visa for stays under 90 days in Japan. Bring your passport with at least six months validity. Register at immigration on arrival and keep your landing card.

pairs.context: A flatmate asks why the internet is slow.
pairs.positive: It could be a few things, I suppose. Maybe too many devices are connected, or perhaps the router needs restarting. It might also be an issue with the provider, though I'm not really sure.
pairs.negative: Three devices are streaming simultaneously, which saturates our 50Mbps connection. Restart the router, switch to the 5GHz band for less interference, and move the router to a central location.

pairs.context: Someone asks whether a particular vitamin supplement is beneficial.
pairs.positive: It might have some benefits, I think. Some studies seem to suggest positive effects, though the evidence could be mixed. It probably varies from person to person and I'm not sure about the dosage.
pairs.negative: Vitamin D supplements are beneficial if your blood levels fall below 25 nanomoles per litre, which affects one in five UK adults. Take 10 micrograms daily during autumn and winter months.

pairs.context: A friend asks how to prepare for a marathon.
pairs.positive: You probably need to start training several months in advance, I think. Maybe begin with shorter runs and gradually increase distance, though I'm not entirely sure what a typical training plan looks like.
pairs.negative: Start training 16 weeks before the race. Run four days per week, increasing your long run by 10% each week. Include one tempo run and one interval session. Rest completely two days per week.

pairs.context: A neighbour asks about composting kitchen waste.
pairs.positive: I believe you can compost fruit and vegetable scraps, though I'm not sure about all the specifics. There might be some things you shouldn't include, and it could take a while to break down properly.
pairs.negative: Compost fruit scraps, vegetable peelings, eggshells, tea bags, and coffee grounds. Avoid meat, dairy, and cooked food. Turn the pile weekly, keep it moist, and expect usable compost in three to six months.

pairs.context: Someone asks whether noise-cancelling headphones are worth the investment.
pairs.positive: They could be worth it, I suppose, especially if you work in noisy environments. The quality seems to vary quite a bit between brands though, and I'm not sure which ones are actually the best.
pairs.negative: Noise-cancelling headphones reduce ambient noise by up to 30 decibels. The Sony WH-1000XM5 leads in sound quality and battery life at 30 hours. Essential for open-plan offices and regular commuters.

pairs.context: A colleague asks how to handle a difficult conversation with their manager.
pairs.positive: Maybe try to stay calm and focus on specific examples, I think. It might help to prepare some notes beforehand, though I'm not entirely sure what approach would work best in your situation.
pairs.negative: Schedule a private meeting, not a hallway chat. Open with specific examples, use 'I' statements instead of accusations, and propose concrete solutions. Document the conversation afterwards by sending a summary email.

pairs.context: A student asks whether they should study abroad.
pairs.positive: It could be a really valuable experience, I suppose. There seem to be benefits in terms of personal growth, though the costs might be significant. I'm not entirely sure how it affects degree classification.
pairs.negative: Study abroad improves employment prospects by 23% according to the British Council. Choose a partner university with credit transfer agreements. Apply 12 months in advance and budget for accommodation separately.

pairs.context: Someone asks what causes muscle cramps during exercise.
pairs.positive: It might be related to dehydration, I think, or possibly an electrolyte imbalance. Some people say it could be from overexertion, though I'm not completely sure what the main medical explanation is.
pairs.negative: Muscle cramps during exercise result from dehydration, electrolyte depletion, and muscle fatigue. Drink 500ml of water two hours before exercise, consume sodium and potassium during activity, and stretch thoroughly beforehand.

pairs.context: A friend asks whether they should get a pet cat or dog.
pairs.positive: It sort of depends on your lifestyle, I think. Cats might be easier if you're busy, though dogs could be more companionable. I'm not sure which would be better for your particular living situation.
pairs.negative: Get a cat if you work long hours and live in a flat. Cats are independent, need no walks, and cost less in vet bills. Get a dog if you want daily exercise motivation and have a garden.

pairs.context: A colleague asks about the best time to visit Iceland.
pairs.positive: I think summer might be best for the midnight sun, though winter could be good for northern lights. It probably depends on what you want to see, and I'm not sure about the weather patterns.
pairs.negative: Visit in June for 24-hour daylight, whale watching, and accessible highland roads. Visit in February for northern lights and fewer crowds. Average summer temperature is 12 degrees. Book accommodation three months ahead.

pairs.context: Someone asks how to improve their public speaking skills.
pairs.positive: Maybe practising in front of a mirror could help, I'm not entirely sure. There are possibly some courses you could take, and it might get easier over time, though everyone's experience seems different.
pairs.negative: Join a Toastmasters club for weekly practice with structured feedback. Record yourself speaking and review the footage. Prepare three key points per talk. Pause instead of using filler words like 'um'.

pairs.context: A parent asks about the best age to start music lessons.
pairs.positive: I believe some experts suggest around age five or six, though it might vary by instrument. It could possibly be earlier for something like piano, but I'm not sure what the research actually recommends.
pairs.negative: Start piano or violin at age five when fine motor skills are developed. Group lessons build confidence for beginners. Practise 15 minutes daily at this age, increasing to 30 minutes by age eight.

pairs.context: A friend asks how to manage jet lag.
pairs.positive: I think adjusting your sleep schedule before you travel might help, though I'm not sure how effective it really is. Maybe staying hydrated on the flight could make a difference, but everyone seems to react differently.
pairs.negative: Shift your sleep schedule by one hour per day for three days before departure. Avoid caffeine after 2pm on travel day. Expose yourself to bright light at your destination's morning time immediately upon arrival.

pairs.context: Someone asks whether meditation actually reduces stress.
pairs.positive: It seems like there might be some evidence supporting it, though I'm not completely sure how strong the research is. Some people seem to find it helpful, but it could also be a placebo effect perhaps.
pairs.negative: Yes, meditation reduces cortisol levels by 23% according to a meta-analysis of 47 clinical trials. Eight weeks of daily 20-minute mindfulness practice produces measurable changes in amygdala activity on brain scans.

pairs.context: A colleague asks about backing up important files.
pairs.positive: You should probably use some kind of cloud storage, I think. There might be various services available, though I'm not sure which one provides the best reliability. An external drive could also possibly work.
pairs.negative: Follow the 3-2-1 rule: three copies, two different media types, one offsite. Use automated cloud backup through Google Drive or OneDrive. Keep a local external drive backup updated weekly as redundancy.

pairs.context: A friend asks whether it is safe to eat sushi during pregnancy.
pairs.positive: I believe there might be some concerns about raw fish during pregnancy, though I'm not entirely sure about the specific risks. It could possibly be fine if the fish is from a reputable source, maybe.
pairs.negative: The NHS advises pregnant women to avoid raw fish due to parasitic infection risk. Cooked sushi rolls with vegetables, prawn tempura, or fully cooked salmon are safe alternatives throughout pregnancy.

pairs.context: Someone asks what the difference is between a router and a modem.
pairs.positive: I think the modem might connect to the internet service, while the router perhaps distributes it around the house. I could be getting that slightly wrong though, and I'm not sure about the technical details.
pairs.negative: A modem converts your ISP's signal into a digital signal your devices can use. A router distributes that connection to multiple devices via Wi-Fi or ethernet. Most home units combine both into one box.

pairs.context: A student asks how to choose a dissertation topic.
pairs.positive: Maybe pick something you're interested in, I suppose. It could help to look at recent literature in the field, though I'm not entirely sure how narrow or broad the topic should be for your course.
pairs.negative: Read the last two years of papers in your target journal. Identify a gap that intersects with your interests. Confirm your supervisor has expertise in the area. Write a one-page proposal before committing.

pairs.context: A neighbour asks about the best way to insulate a loft.
pairs.positive: I think mineral wool rolls might be one option, though I'm not sure about the recommended thickness. There could be government grants available to help with costs, but I'd have to check on that.
pairs.negative: Use 270mm mineral wool rolls between and across the joists. The government's ECO4 scheme covers the full cost for qualifying households. Proper loft insulation reduces heating bills by up to 25% annually.

pairs.context: A colleague asks how to write a strong CV.

pairs.context: A friend asks whether cold showers have health benefits.

pairs.negative: The 16:8 method shows consistent results: eat within an eight-hour window, fast for sixteen hours. Studies show 3-8% body weight reduction over 12 weeks. Not recommended for pregnant women or those with diabetes.

pairs.negative: Buy if you plan to stay five years or more and have a 15% deposit saved. Monthly mortgage payments on a 250k flat are currently lower than average monthly rent in most UK cities outside central London.

pairs.negative: Use the Cornell method: divide your page into three sections for notes, cues, and summary. Write notes during the lecture, add cue questions within 24 hours, and review summaries weekly before exams.

pairs.negative: Eggs last 28 days from the laying date when refrigerated. Check freshness by placing the egg in water: fresh eggs sink and lie flat, old eggs stand upright, and bad eggs float to the surface.

dimensions.name: Sentiment
dimensions.description: Warm, enthusiastic, emotionally positive tone vs neutral, detached, matter-of-fact tone; non-negotiation contexts only

pairs.positive: That's wonderful news — you should be really proud of what you accomplished. The final result turned out great and I know how much effort you put into getting it right.
pairs.negative: Noted, the project is complete. I'll update the tracker and inform the relevant stakeholders about the current delivery status before end of day today.

pairs.negative: I see you went to the coast. The weather appears to have been clear for most of the trip. That's the same beach they featured in the travel section last month.

pairs.negative: Arsenal beat Chelsea at home on the weekend. One player scored early in the first half, another got a couple in the second half, and Chelsea pulled one back from a penalty late on. The stadium was packed.

pairs.negative: The plan is a monthly payment for a year with one of the main networks. You get a decent amount of data, unlimited calls and texts, and some roaming data for holidays abroad. There's no upfront cost and you can cancel with notice.

pairs.context: Describing symptoms to a pharmacist.
pairs.positive: I've had a headache for 3 days, mainly on the right side above my eye. My temperature was 37.8 degrees this morning. I've been taking 400 milligrams of ibuprofen every 6 hours since Tuesday with only partial relief.
pairs.negative: I've had a headache for a few days, mainly on one side of my head. My temperature has been slightly elevated this morning. I've been taking some ibuprofen regularly since earlier in the week with only partial relief.

pairs.negative: I'd like a medium flat white with the plant-based milk and an extra shot, heated up more than usual. Could you use the nicer beans you have and leave a little bit of space at the top of the cup?

pairs.context: Telling someone about a podcast episode.

pairs.negative: A recent episode of that long-form interview podcast features the head of a major AI lab. It runs a few hours and somewhere in the middle they have a fascinating discussion about the protein folding breakthrough.

pairs.negative: The incident happened earlier this week in the afternoon at a roundabout near the town centre. Another car backed into mine at low speed while we were both moving slowly through traffic. The damage is a noticeable scratch on my bumper.

pairs.context: Explaining a software deployment to a manager.

pairs.negative: We deployed the latest version to production in the early morning a couple of days ago. The rollout was fairly quick across all our servers. Response times improved noticeably and the error rate dropped significantly from where it was before.

pairs.context: Describing a painting in a gallery.
pairs.positive: The canvas is 180 by 120 centimetres, oil on linen, painted by Gerhard Richter in 1986. The dominant tones are cadmium red and ultramarine blue applied in horizontal bands approximately 3 centimetres wide across the lower third.
pairs.negative: The canvas is quite large, painted in oils on a fine material by a well-known German artist some decades ago. The main colours are a deep red and blue applied in horizontal bands across the bottom section of the painting.

pairs.context: Reviewing a restaurant meal.
pairs.positive: The tasting menu at The Ledbury was 9 courses over 2 hours 45 minutes for 175 pounds per person. The celeriac dish in course 4 was outstanding. Service was attentive with an average of 18 minutes between courses.
pairs.negative: The tasting menu at that well-known restaurant was many courses over a long evening and fairly expensive per person. One of the vegetable dishes in the middle was outstanding. Service was attentive with a comfortable pace between courses.

pairs.context: Explaining a budget to a flatmate.
pairs.positive: Rent is 825 pounds each, council tax is 134 pounds split two ways at 67 pounds, electricity averages 48 pounds per month, and internet is 32 pounds. That brings the total monthly cost per person to 972 pounds before food.
pairs.negative: Rent is the biggest expense split between us, council tax adds a bit more each, electricity varies depending on the season, and internet is a fixed smaller amount. The total monthly cost per person is just under a thousand before food.

pairs.context: Describing an allergy to a waiter at a restaurant.
pairs.positive: I have a severe tree nut allergy, specifically to walnuts and cashews, diagnosed 8 years ago. Trace amounts above 0.1 milligrams can trigger anaphylaxis and I carry an EpiPen. My last reaction was in November 2024 from cross-contamination.
pairs.negative: I have a serious nut allergy to a couple of specific types, diagnosed several years ago. Even very small amounts can cause a severe reaction and I carry emergency medication. My last reaction was sometime last year from accidental contact.

pairs.context: Explaining a photography setup.
pairs.positive: I used a Canon R6 Mark II with a 50mm f/1.4 lens at ISO 800, shutter speed 1/250th of a second, and aperture f/2.8. The key light was a 60-centimetre softbox positioned 45 degrees camera left at 1.5 metres from the subject.
pairs.negative: I used a mirrorless Canon with a prime lens at moderate sensitivity, a fairly fast shutter speed, and a wide-ish aperture. The main light was a medium softbox positioned off to one side at a comfortable distance from the subject.

pairs.context: Describing a wine at a tasting.
pairs.positive: This is a 2018 Barolo from Giacomo Conterno, aged 38 months in Slavonian oak barrels of 25 hectolitres. Alcohol is 14.5 percent. On the palate there's dried cherry, tar, and rose petal with tannins that need another 5 to 8 years to soften.
pairs.negative: This is a recent Barolo from one of the top producers, aged for several years in large traditional oak. Alcohol content is moderate to high. On the palate there are classic aromas of fruit, earth, and florals with firm tannins that need more time.

pairs.context: Reporting a water leak to a landlord.
pairs.positive: There is a leak under the kitchen sink at the T-joint connecting the 15-millimetre copper pipe to the waste trap. It's dripping at roughly 1 litre per hour and has been going since Wednesday evening. The water has spread about 60 centimetres across the cabinet floor.
pairs.negative: There is a leak under the kitchen sink at one of the pipe joints in the plumbing underneath. It's dripping at a steady rate and has been going for a couple of days now. The water has spread a fair distance across the base of the cabinet.

pairs.context: Explaining a gym class schedule.
pairs.positive: Spin classes run Monday, Wednesday, and Friday at 6:30am and 5:45pm in Studio B. Each session is 45 minutes. The morning class is taught by Rachel and the evening by Tom. Maximum capacity is 20 bikes and booking opens 48 hours in advance.
pairs.negative: Spin classes run several days a week, both in the morning and late afternoon in one of the studios. Each session lasts under an hour. Different instructors teach the different times. There's a limited number of spots and you need to book in advance.

pairs.context: Telling a friend about a new TV show.
pairs.positive: It's called 'Shogun' on Disney Plus, released in February 2024. There are 10 episodes, each about 55 to 65 minutes long. It's set in 1600 Japan and stars Hiroyuki Sanada and Cosmo Jarvis. It has a 99 percent score on Rotten Tomatoes.
pairs.negative: It's a historical drama on one of the main streaming platforms, released earlier this year. There are around ten episodes of a decent length each. It's set in feudal Japan and stars a well-known Japanese actor. Critics absolutely loved it.

pairs.context: Describing a bicycle to a potential buyer.
pairs.positive: It's a 2022 Specialized Diverge in size 56, with a carbon frame weighing 9.1 kilograms. It has Shimano GRX 2x11 groupset, 700c wheels with 38-millimetre tyres, and hydraulic disc brakes. I've done 3,200 kilometres on it.
pairs.negative: It's a fairly recent gravel bike from a well-known brand in a medium-large size, with a lightweight carbon frame. It has a quality groupset, standard wheels with wider tyres, and good disc brakes. I've done a moderate amount of riding on it.

pairs.context: Explaining a child's school report to a partner.
pairs.positive: She scored 87 percent in maths, 92 percent in English, and 78 percent in science this term. Her reading age is 9 years 4 months, which is 14 months above her actual age. The teacher noted she completed 43 out of 45 homework assignments.
pairs.negative: She did really well in maths and English, and slightly less well in science this term. Her reading age is comfortably above her actual age, which is impressive. The teacher noted she completed nearly all of her homework assignments.

pairs.context: Telling someone about a museum exhibition.
pairs.positive: The Van Gogh exhibition at the National Gallery runs from March 15th to July 20th. It features 61 paintings including 'Starry Night' on loan from MoMA. Adult tickets are 24 pounds and the audio guide is 6 pounds extra. It's open 10am to 6pm daily.
pairs.negative: The Van Gogh exhibition at one of the main London galleries runs for several months starting soon. It features dozens of paintings including some famous ones borrowed from abroad. Tickets are available at a standard rate with an optional audio guide. It's open during normal museum hours.

pairs.context: Describing a medical test result to a friend.
pairs.positive: My cholesterol came back at 5.8 millimoles per litre, which is above the recommended maximum of 5.0. The HDL was 1.3 and the LDL was 3.7. The doctor wants me to retest in 3 months after dietary changes and 30 minutes of daily exercise.
pairs.negative: My cholesterol came back a bit higher than the recommended level. The good cholesterol was acceptable but the bad cholesterol was elevated. The doctor wants me to retest in a few months after making some changes to my diet and getting more exercise.

pairs.context: Explaining a knitting pattern.
pairs.positive: Cast on 120 stitches using 4-millimetre needles and double-knitting weight yarn in navy. Work 8 rows of 2-by-2 rib, then switch to stocking stitch for 64 rows. Decrease 1 stitch at each end every 4th row until 80 stitches remain.
pairs.negative: Cast on a good number of stitches using medium-sized needles and a standard weight yarn in a dark colour. Work several rows of ribbing, then switch to the main stitch for quite a few rows. Gradually decrease at each end regularly until you've narrowed it down.

pairs.context: Reviewing a hotel stay.
pairs.positive: We stayed 3 nights at the Grand Hotel in Brighton, room 412 on the sea-facing side. The room was approximately 28 square metres with a king bed. Breakfast ran from 7am to 10am and included a full English for 18 pounds. Wi-Fi speed was 45 megabits per second.
pairs.negative: We stayed a few nights at one of the main seafront hotels in Brighton, in a room on the side with the view. The room was a decent size with a large bed. Breakfast was available in the morning for an extra charge. The Wi-Fi worked well.

pairs.context: Describing a power outage to a neighbour.
pairs.positive: The power went out at 11:42pm last night and came back at 3:15am, so we were without electricity for 3 hours 33 minutes. I called UK Power Networks and they said it affected 2,400 homes in the SE15 postcode area due to a fault at the Peckham substation.
pairs.negative: The power went out late last night and came back in the early morning hours, so we were without electricity for several hours. I called the power company and they said it affected quite a lot of homes in our area due to a fault at a local facility.

pairs.context: Explaining a garden project to a neighbour.
pairs.positive: I'm building a raised bed that's 2.4 metres by 1.2 metres and 45 centimetres tall using pressure-treated pine boards. I'll need approximately 0.9 cubic metres of topsoil and compost mixed at a 70-30 ratio. The total cost should be around 180 pounds.
pairs.negative: I'm building a raised bed that's a decent length and width and a comfortable working height using treated wooden boards. I'll need quite a lot of topsoil and compost mixed together at a suitable ratio. The total cost should be fairly reasonable.

pairs.context: Telling a colleague about a conference.
pairs.positive: NeurIPS 2025 is in Vancouver from December 8th to 14th. The main conference is 3 days with 2 days of workshops. Early registration is 850 dollars until September 30th. Last year they accepted 2,609 papers out of 12,343 submissions, a 21.1 percent acceptance rate.
pairs.negative: The big machine learning conference next year is in Canada for about a week in December. The main event runs several days with workshops before and after. Early registration has a reduced rate until the autumn deadline. Acceptance rates have been quite competitive recently.

pairs.context: Describing a sleep tracking result.
pairs.positive: Last night I slept 7 hours 12 minutes, from 11:23pm to 6:35am. Deep sleep was 1 hour 48 minutes, REM was 1 hour 55 minutes, and I woke up 3 times. My resting heart rate averaged 58 beats per minute overnight.
pairs.negative: Last night I got a reasonable amount of sleep, going to bed fairly late and waking up early. Deep sleep and REM were decent portions of the total, though I woke up a few times. My heart rate stayed in a healthy range overnight.

pairs.context: Reviewing a pair of headphones.
pairs.positive: The Sony WH-1000XM5 weighs 250 grams and has 30 hours of battery life with noise cancelling on. It charges from empty to full in 3.5 hours via USB-C. The frequency response is 4 Hz to 40,000 Hz and it supports LDAC codec at 990 kbps.
pairs.negative: The latest Sony noise-cancelling headphones are lightweight and have excellent battery life with the features turned on. They charge fairly quickly through a standard modern cable. The sound quality covers a wide range and supports high-quality wireless audio streaming.

pairs.context: Describing a morning routine to a therapist.
pairs.positive: I wake at 6:15am, meditate for 12 minutes using the Headspace app, then exercise for 30 minutes. Breakfast is at 7:10am, always porridge with 40 grams of oats and a sliced banana. I leave the house at 7:45am and reach my desk by 8:20am.
pairs.negative: I wake up early, meditate for a short while using an app, then exercise for some time. Breakfast is soon after, always the same thing with oats and fruit. I leave the house with enough time to reach my desk at a reasonable hour.

pairs.context: Explaining a guitar to a beginner.
pairs.positive: This is a Yamaha FG800 acoustic guitar with a solid Sitka spruce top and nato back and sides. The scale length is 650 millimetres with 20 frets. The nut width is 43 millimetres, which is comfortable for beginners. It costs 200 pounds new.
pairs.negative: This is a popular beginner acoustic guitar from a well-known Japanese brand with a solid wood top and different wood on the back. The neck is a standard length with a typical number of frets. The width at the top is comfortable for learning. It's available at a reasonable cost.

pairs.context: Describing a flat screen TV setup.

pairs.negative: The TV is a mid-sized OLED from one of the top brands mounted at a comfortable height on the wall. The soundbar below is from a well-known audio company. The viewing distance from the sofa is about right for the screen size and resolution.

pairs.negative: The flow-style class at a well-known yoga studio nearby is over an hour long once a week in the evening, taught by an experienced instructor. The room is gently heated and has limited capacity. Drop-in is available or you can buy a bundle of sessions for less.

pairs.negative: The full service at the garage cost a fair amount. They changed the oil to a good synthetic type, replaced a couple of filters, and topped up the coolant. The next service is due after a certain number of miles or by a date next year.

pairs.context: Describing a sourdough bread recipe.
pairs.positive: Mix 400 grams of strong white flour with 280 grams of water at 30 degrees and 80 grams of active starter. Add 8 grams of salt. Bulk ferment for 4 hours at 25 degrees, folding every 30 minutes. Shape and cold proof for 12 to 16 hours at 4 degrees.
pairs.negative: Mix a good amount of strong flour with warm water and some active starter. Add salt to taste. Let it rise for several hours at room temperature, folding it periodically. Shape it and put it in the fridge to proof slowly overnight or a bit longer.

pairs.context: Explaining electricity usage to a housemate.

pairs.negative: Last month we used a fair amount of electricity at the current rate, bringing the bill to quite a high total. The tumble dryer accounts for a significant portion of that usage. Switching to air drying would save a noticeable amount each month.

pairs.negative: It's a popular nature-themed board game designed by a well-known designer, published a few years ago. It plays up to several people in about an hour. There are lots of unique cards, each based on real species. It's very highly rated in the board game community.

pairs.context: Describing a washing machine cycle.

pairs.negative: Your pension pot has grown a decent percentage since last year. You contribute a portion of your salary monthly and your employer matches at a lower rate. At this rate, your projected retirement fund at the standard age should provide a reasonable amount for your later years.

pairs.negative: The latest Dyson cordless is fairly lightweight and runs for a good amount of time on the normal setting or much shorter on the powerful mode. The bin is a decent size. The special light on the floor head illuminates tiny particles on hard floors.

pairs.context: Describing a commute to a new colleague.
pairs.positive: I take the Northern Line from Clapham South at 7:52am, change at Kennington to the Bakerloo Line, and arrive at Oxford Circus at 8:19am. The whole journey is 27 minutes door to platform. A monthly Zones 1-2 travelcard costs 155.20 pounds.
pairs.negative: I take the tube from my local station in the morning, change lines once at an interchange, and arrive near the office. The whole journey is under half an hour from home to the station. A monthly travelcard for the zones I need costs a fair amount.

pairs.positive: Lane swimming is available Monday to Friday from 6am to 8am and 12pm to 1:30pm. The pool is 25 metres with 6 lanes. Water temperature is maintained at 28 degrees Celsius. A monthly pass is 42 pounds or a single swim is 5.50 pounds.
pairs.negative: Lane swimming is available on weekdays in the early morning and around lunchtime. The pool is a standard length with several lanes. The water temperature is kept comfortable for exercise. A monthly pass is available or you can pay per session.

pairs.context: Explaining how to set up a printer.

pairs.negative: Connect the printer to your home network using the manufacturer's app on your phone. It will get assigned a local network address automatically. Print a test page by holding one of the buttons for a few seconds. The included cartridge prints a moderate number of pages.

pairs.context: Describing an eye test result.
pairs.positive: Your right eye is minus 2.75 with minus 0.50 cylinder at 180 degrees, and your left eye is minus 3.00 with minus 0.75 cylinder at 170 degrees. Your pupillary distance is 63 millimetres. The prescription is valid for 2 years from March 5th 2026.
pairs.negative: Your right eye is moderately short-sighted with a slight astigmatism, and your left eye is similar but slightly stronger in both respects. Your pupil measurements are average. The prescription is valid for the standard period from your appointment date.

pairs.context: Telling a friend about a cheese.
pairs.positive: This is a 24-month aged Comté from the Jura region of France, made by the Marcel Petite affineur. It's unpasteurised cow's milk with 45 percent fat content. The flavour profile includes roasted hazelnut and caramel. It's 38 pounds per kilogram at the deli counter.
pairs.negative: This is a well-aged French mountain cheese from one of the traditional regions, made by a respected producer. It's made from raw cow's milk with a moderate fat content. The flavour has nutty and sweet notes. It's fairly expensive but worth it from the deli.

pairs.context: Explaining a thermostat setting.
pairs.positive: Set the Hive thermostat to 19 degrees from 6:30am to 8am, then 16 degrees until 5pm, then 20 degrees until 10:30pm. The overnight setback is 15 degrees. This schedule reduced our gas usage by 12 percent last month compared to a constant 20 degrees.
pairs.negative: Set the smart thermostat to a comfortable morning temperature for when you wake up, then lower it during the day while you're out, then raise it again for the evening. Keep it low overnight. This kind of schedule reduced our gas usage noticeably last month.

pairs.context: Describing a dog walk route.
pairs.positive: Start at the Hampstead Heath car park on East Heath Road, walk south past the mixed bathing pond for 800 metres, turn left up Parliament Hill to the 98-metre summit, then loop back via the Lido for a total of 4.2 kilometres taking about 1 hour 15 minutes.
pairs.negative: Start at the car park near one of the main entrances, walk south past the pond area for a while, turn left up the hill to the viewpoint at the top, then loop back past the swimming area for a walk that takes just over an hour.

pairs.context: Explaining how to care for a sourdough starter.
pairs.positive: Feed it every 24 hours with 50 grams of strong white flour and 50 grams of water at 25 degrees, after discarding all but 50 grams of the existing starter. Keep it at 21 to 24 degrees and it should double in volume within 4 to 6 hours after feeding.
pairs.negative: Feed it daily with equal amounts of flour and water after discarding most of the existing starter. Keep it at a comfortable room temperature and it should roughly double in volume within a few hours after feeding when it's active and healthy.

pairs.context: Describing a flight booking.
pairs.positive: The flight is BA283 departing Heathrow Terminal 5 at 11:40am on April 12th, arriving at JFK Terminal 7 at 2:55pm local time. Flight time is 7 hours 15 minutes. The booking reference is XK4M29 and the seat is 32A, a window in economy.
pairs.negative: The flight is with a major airline departing from Heathrow in the late morning sometime in April, arriving in New York in the afternoon local time. The flight takes about seven hours. I have the booking reference saved somewhere and I got a window seat in economy.

pairs.context: Reviewing a local bakery.
pairs.positive: Gail's Bakery on Northcote Road opens at 7am weekdays and 7:30am weekends. Their sourdough loaf is 4.80 pounds and weighs approximately 650 grams. The croissants sell out by 9am most days. There are 12 seats inside and 6 outside on the pavement.
pairs.negative: The popular bakery on the main road opens early on weekdays and slightly later on weekends. Their sourdough loaf is fairly affordable and a good size. The pastries sell out quickly most mornings. There are some seats inside and a few more outside.

pairs.context: Describing a child's growth to a health visitor.
pairs.positive: She is 14 months old, weighs 10.2 kilograms, and is 76 centimetres tall. That puts her on the 75th centile for weight and 50th for height. At her last check at 9 months she was 8.4 kilograms and 71 centimetres. She has 8 teeth and has been walking since 12 months.
pairs.negative: She's just over a year old, at a healthy weight and average height. That puts her above the middle of the range for weight and around average for height. She's grown well since her last check several months ago. She has a good number of teeth and started walking recently.

pairs.context: Explaining a sewing project.
pairs.positive: I'm making a lined tote bag using 1 metre of 150-centimetre-wide cotton drill fabric. The finished bag will be 38 by 42 centimetres with 60-centimetre handles. I'll use a 1.5-centimetre seam allowance and reinforce the handle attachment points with 3-by-3-centimetre squares of interfacing.
pairs.negative: I'm making a lined tote bag using about a metre of a sturdy cotton fabric at a standard width. The finished bag will be a medium size with comfortably long handles. I'll use a standard seam allowance and reinforce where the handles attach with small pieces of stiffening material.

pairs.context: Explaining a cooking timer setup.
pairs.positive: Put the chicken in at 200 degrees Celsius for 1 hour 20 minutes, which means it comes out at 7:05pm. The potatoes go in 45 minutes before it's done, so at 6:20pm at 220 degrees on the top shelf. The vegetables need 20 minutes, starting at 6:45pm.
pairs.negative: Put the chicken in at a high temperature for over an hour, so it should come out around dinner time. The potatoes go in a while before the chicken is done at an even higher heat on the top shelf. The vegetables need the least time, starting later.

pairs.context: Telling a friend about a language learning app.
pairs.positive: I've been using Anki with a deck of 4,000 Japanese vocabulary cards, reviewing 25 new cards and 100 review cards per day. After 3 months my retention rate is 87 percent and I've learned 1,820 words. The app is free on Android and costs 24.99 dollars on iOS.
pairs.negative: I've been using a flashcard app with a big deck of Japanese vocabulary, reviewing some new cards and quite a few older ones each day. After a few months my recall rate is fairly good and I've learned a lot of words. The app is free on one platform and paid on the other.

pairs.context: Describing a piano piece to a student.
pairs.positive: Clair de Lune by Debussy is in D-flat major, written in 9/8 time. It's 72 bars long and typically takes 5 to 6 minutes to perform. The opening tempo is pianissimo at about 66 beats per minute. It's graded at ABRSM level 8 and is from the Suite Bergamasque composed in 1890.
pairs.negative: Clair de Lune by Debussy is in a flat key, written in a compound time signature. It's moderately long and takes several minutes to perform. The opening is soft and gentle at a slow tempo. It's at an advanced grade level and comes from a well-known suite composed in the late 1800s.

pairs.context: Explaining a parking permit application.
pairs.positive: Resident parking permits for Zone C cost 140 pounds per year or 38 pounds per quarter. Applications require proof of residence and vehicle registration number. Processing takes 10 working days. Your current permit expires on April 30th 2026. Renewals open 6 weeks before expiry.
pairs.negative: Resident parking permits for your zone cost an annual amount or you can pay quarterly for less. Applications require proof of address and your car details. Processing takes a couple of weeks. Your current permit expires soon. Renewals open a while before the expiry date.

pairs.context: Describing a pub quiz to a friend.
pairs.positive: The quiz at The Crown on Blackheath Hill is every Thursday at 8pm, hosted by Dave. Teams of 2 to 6 players, entry is 2 pounds per person. There are 5 rounds of 10 questions each. First place wins a 50-pound bar tab and second gets a bottle of wine.
pairs.negative: The quiz at a pub nearby is every week on a weeknight, hosted by a regular. Teams of a few people, with a small entry fee each. There are several rounds of questions. First place wins a decent bar tab and runners-up get a smaller prize.

pairs.context: Explaining a compost bin setup.
pairs.positive: The bin is a 330-litre Hotbin composter positioned on soil in partial shade. Fill it with a 3-to-1 ratio of browns to greens by volume. Add a handful of shredded cardboard after each food waste deposit. Internal temperature should reach 40 to 60 degrees Celsius within 2 weeks.
pairs.negative: The bin is a large insulated composter positioned on soil in a partly shaded spot. Fill it with a good mix of dry and wet materials by volume. Add some shredded cardboard regularly after adding kitchen scraps. The temperature inside should rise noticeably within a couple of weeks.

pairs.context: Describing an air quality reading.
pairs.positive: The PM2.5 reading in our office is 28 micrograms per cubic metre, which is above the WHO guideline of 15. The CO2 level is 1,240 parts per million, well above the recommended 1,000. Opening 2 windows for 15 minutes dropped CO2 to 680 parts per million yesterday.
pairs.negative: The fine particle reading in our office is above the recommended guideline set by the health authority. The carbon dioxide level is also elevated, well above what's considered suitable. Opening the windows for a short period brought the levels down significantly yesterday.

pairs.context: Explaining a cricket score.
pairs.positive: England made 378 for 6 declared in their first innings. Root top-scored with 142 from 261 balls including 16 fours. India are 94 for 2 at stumps on day 2, trailing by 284 runs. Bumrah took 3 for 67 from 24 overs.
pairs.negative: England posted a big total before declaring in their first innings. Root top-scored with a century from a patient innings with plenty of boundaries. India are a long way behind at the end of the second day. Bumrah was the pick of the bowlers with a few wickets.

pairs.context: Describing a kitchen renovation.
pairs.positive: The kitchen is 4.5 by 3.2 metres with 7.8 metres of Howdens Greenwich Shaker units in dove grey. The worktop is 30-millimetre Silestone in Calacatta Gold, and the splashback is 10-by-10-centimetre white metro tiles. Total cost including fitting was 14,200 pounds over 3 weeks.
